Name: S0003 -Piloting a Private-Public Genotyping Consortium for Crops in LMICs
Dscription: Piloting a Private-Public Genotyping Consortium for Crops in LMICs
Project Summary: The ShelfPlus – Reducing Millet Rancidity through Gene Editing project is a CIMMYT-led initiative funded by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (BMGF) to enhance the shelf-life and nutritional value of millet flour. The project focuses on gene editing technologies to mitigate rancidity in milled millet, a key challenge that reduces its storage potential and marketability. By identifying and modifying genetic pathways that influence lipid oxidation, the initiative aims to improve millet’s post-harvest stability, ensuring longer usability for consumers and processors. CIMMYT collaborates with breeding institutions, food scientists, and regulatory bodies to facilitate the development and adoption of these innovations. The project includes capacity-building efforts, regulatory compliance guidance, and knowledge dissemination to promote the responsible use of gene editing in food systems. By extending millet’s shelf life, ShelfPlus supports food security, market competitiveness, and economic opportunities for smallholder farmers and agri-food industries.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: N-344002- BIOTECHNOLOGY INNOVATIONS FOR FOOD SECURITY: AN ENABLING POLICY PLATFORM
D-200394-Program 2- International host / pathogen Differential Sets:
Dscription: Program 2- International host / pathogen Differential Sets: Effective Genetic and Sustinable Management of Ascochyta Blight of Chickpea - Phase 2
Project Summary:
TRANSFORMED aims to support and promote a largescale adoption of successful agroforestry systems (AFS) in saline and degraded areas to restore soil health, increase biodiversity and productivity of the agroecosystems by overcoming the identified barriers for large-scale implementation.
The project raises awareness and facilitates knowledge transfer and capacity building of the potential of AFS for soil restoration and productivity for farmers and farmers associations, researchers, NGOs, private sector and policy makers. In addition, the project develops and strengthens governance arrangements at community level while co-developing a knowledge sharing and innovation upscaling strategy capitalizing on the existing and new knowledge created to maximize the successful adoption of AFS and a broad multistakeholder support. TRANSFORMED will build up on already AFS designs practiced by pioneer farmers or investigated at on-farm research trials that have been proven to increase the productivity of highly degraded land and improve soil health.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: T-PJ-004023-VACS-Breeding: TARO
Dscription: PJ-004023: VACS-Breeding: TARO
Project Summary: This project aims to enhance agrifood systems by developing and delivering productive, climate-resilient and nutrient-dense taro varieties in Nigeria, Ghana, Ethiopia and Mozambique. The primary objectives are to boost productivity, improve nutritional content and commercialize climate-resilient taro varieties for smallholder farmers. Key outputs of this project include defined taro market segments, established breeding networks, detailed germplasm reports and robust breeding systems. Additionally, this project will produce a significant quantity of seeds, train numerous individuals and strengthen market linkages among taro farmers. Activities in this project include conducting workshops, identifying market segments, developing product profiles and characterizing taro germplasm.

Name: T-PJ-004017-VACS-Breeding: Bambara
Dscription: PJ-004017: VACS-Breeding: Bambara
Project Summary: This project aims to enhance the development and dissemination of Bambara groundnut varieties through a gender-responsive approach that prioritizes nutrition traits. By establishing the PAN African Bambara Breeding Network (PABBN), the initiative will facilitate collaboration and knowledge sharing across regions. Germplasm development will be guided by target product profiles, ensuring that the resulting varieties meet the specific needs of different communities, thereby improving food security and resilience across Africa.
Project results: Establish Bambara breeding networks, defined market segments, target product profiles, set up breeding systems (BMS) and characterized germplasm. To also deliver MET, on-farm and DUS reports, produce seeds, train personnel, submit progress reports, analyze sales data, strengthen market linkages and release high-yielding, resilient and nutrient-dense Bambara varieties for commercialization.

Name: P-1520-GOO0-Sustainable Intensification of Potato for Achiev
Dscription: Sustainable Intensification of Potato for Achieving Self-Sufficiency in Odisha
Project Summary:
Project objective: to establish a sustainable local seed production supply chain and scale up SFLF group farming to cover key potato growing districts. Our ambitious 2026 target is to double the potato acreage from 37,000 acres to 74,000 acres, with additional production of nearly 400,000 tons both though additional acreage and improved productivity on existing acreage. 
CIP, in partnership with the DoH, aims at the following specific objectives:
- Expand SFLF group potato cultivation to cover 5,100 acres by the end of 2026.
- Organize exposure visits for farmers to SFLF sites, facilitate use of quality seeds and improved production practices on an additional 10,000 acres by 2026.
- Sustainable Intensification of Potato for Achieving Self-Sufficiency in OdishaProject report (December 2022–March 2024)
- Expand local seed production through apical rooted cuttings and net-house seed multiplications to produce 30–50 percent of the state's seed requirements.
- Strengthen capacity of potato growers, horticultural staff, and scientists, in seed, processing, and table potato production-related technologies.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: T-PJ-003255-Building Economically Sustainable Seed Systems f
Dscription: Building Economically Sustainable Seed Systems for Cassava (BASICS-II)
Project results: The BASICS-II Project successfully strengthened cassava seed systems in Nigeria and Tanzania, establishing sustainable seed enterprises, supporting decentralized cassava seed entrepreneurs (CSEs), and engaging processors in seed production. Policies and regulatory frameworks were enhanced, and new varieties gained rapid adoption. Collaboration across partners ensured quality control and market linkages. The model was extended to Sierra Leone and Liberia, with SeedTracker playing a key role in monitoring and sustainability efforts.

Name: A1172Crops to End Hunger
Dscription: Crops to End Hunger
Project Summary: The CGIAR system aims to advance sustainable development goals, especially SDG 2, which targets ending hunger and promoting sustainable agriculture. The "11 Crops to End Hunger" initiative seeks to enhance CGIAR’s crop breeding effectiveness by partnering with national agricultural research systems (NARS) to develop and deliver high-yielding, resilient, and nutritious staple crops for smallholder farmers. This initiative focuses on leveraging CGIAR’s strengths in plant breeding and crop biodiversity conservation. By introducing superior crop varieties, the program aims to boost productivity, resilience, and profitability, addressing both consumer preferences and climate challenges while continually improving genetic gain in agriculture.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo
Funder: Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit / German Society for International Cooperation

Name: BIUSA (Bezos Earth Fund)-Using genetic diversity to capture carbon through deep root systems in tropical soils  (Research and Capacity development)
Dscription:
Both description and project summary both filled. The “Using Genetic Diversity to Capture Carbon Through Deep Root Systems in Tropical Soils” project focuses on leveraging genetic diversity to develop new root ideotypes of tropical forage grass and rice. The goal is to enhance carbon assimilation via photosynthesis and deep rooting ability, contributing to increased carbon storage in tropical soils. The project aims to support the completion and maintenance of the Future Seeds facility, develop novel high-throughput phenotyping methods to evaluate genetic diversity in deep rooting ability, and build capacity for conducting research on root systems and soil carbon storage in tropical regions. Scope and Activities:
1. Development and Phenotyping:
o Develop high-throughput phenotyping methods to screen and evaluate genetic diversity in root systems of tropical forage grass and rice.
o Identify and improve forage and rice genotypes with deep rooting traits to enhance soil organic carbon (SOC) storage.
2. Research and Innovation:
o Conduct field trials to test the performance of new genotypes in different agroecological niches.
o Apply gene editing technologies to improve root traits and photosynthetic efficiency.
3. Capacity Building and Dissemination:
o Train young researchers in genetic diversity and carbon farming.
o Develop and implement strategies to disseminate improved root ideotypes to farmers in tropical regions.
4. Impact and Monitoring:
o Monitor the impact of deep-rooted genotypes on SOC storage and agricultural productivity.
o Evaluate the benefits of carbon removal from the atmosphere and its implications for climate change mitigation.
Project Summary: The "Using Genetic Diversity to Capture Carbon Through Deep Root Systems in Tropical Soils" project aims to enhance carbon storage in tropical soils by developing new root ideotypes of tropical forage grasses and rice. By leveraging genetic diversity, the project seeks to improve carbon assimilation and deep rooting traits, contributing to increased soil organic carbon storage. The project will develop high-throughput phenotyping methods, conduct field trials, and apply gene editing technologies to enhance root systems. Additionally, it aims to build research capacity and disseminate improved genotypes to farmers. Expected outcomes include improved soil health, increased agricultural productivity, and significant contributions to climate change mitigation through enhanced carbon sequestration.
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Dscription: VACS Crop Improvement Coalition
Project Summary: The VACS project aims to enhance agricultural productivity and nutrition by developing climate-resilient crop varieties and building healthy soils. Its strategic goals are to transform agrifood systems through innovation and inclusivity, ensure food and nutrition security within planetary boundaries, and improve the resilience of smallholder farmers' livelihoods. VACS focuses on developing resilient crop varieties, enhancing soil health, supporting smallholder farmers, advocating for supportive policies, and monitoring and evaluating impact. The expected outcomes are increased productivity, improved nutrition, resilient livelihoods, sustainable agrifood systems, and strengthened global coordination to address agrifood disruptions and climate impacts.

The project focuses on revitalizing traditional crops like pigeon pea, which are crucial for improving soil health and providing balanced nutrition.

Name: D-200390-UK-CGIAR Collaboration: Leveraging genetic innovations for a
Dscription: UK-CGIAR Collaboration: Leveraging genetic innovations for accelerated breeding of climate resilient and nutritious crop
Project Summary: This project aims to accelerate the breeding process and deliver higher genetic gain, , farmer-preferred varieties by adopting new breeding approaches (genome editing and sequence-based gene discovery), exploiting novel genetic variation and developing data-driven approaches to breeding. While the focus is on wheat, the methods and pipelines can be employed to other crops. The project team use genome editing to develop locally adapted wheat varieties with (i) enhanced resistance to wheat rusts; increased iron content, whilst also developing ‘big genome data’ approaches to accelerate genetic gain in traditional breeding approaches. The project works to reduce reliance on imports in major wheat importing countries (e.g., Egypt is top globally), improved resilience to climate-change triggered rust epidemics, increased iron intake and improvement of women and children’s health in societies where wheat is a staple crop (particularly Pakistan, Egypt, Kenya).
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: Climate-smart initiatives for climate change adaptation and sustainability in prioritized agricultural production systems in Colombia (CSICAP)
Dscription: The Climate-Smart Initiatives for Climate Change Adaptation and Sustainability in Prioritized Agricultural Production Systems in Colombia (CSICAP) project aims to safeguard livelihoods, boost productivity, and enhance profitability in nine of Colombia's key agri-food value chains, while increasing resilience to climate change impacts and reducing greenhouse gas emissions in the agriculture sector. With a USD $100 million budget, funded by the Green Climate Fund (GCF), Development Bank of Latin America (CAF), the government of Colombia, and partner contributions, CSICAP aims to benefit 200,000 people directly and nearly one million indirectly. The project will help reduce over 2 million tons of CO2 equivalent emissions through digital agroclimatic services, climate-resilient crops, water-efficient climate-smart practices, inclusive capacity-building, and innovative business models. It will be implemented in 22 out of 32 departments in Colombia.
Project Summary: The Climate-Smart Initiatives for Climate Change Adaptation and Sustainability in Prioritized Agricultural Production Systems in Colombia (CSICAP) project aims to safeguard livelihoods, boost productivity, and enhance profitability in nine of Colombia's key agri-food value chains, while increasing resilience to climate change impacts and reducing greenhouse gas emissions in the agriculture sector. Name: T-PJ-003917-Strengthening soybean crop improvement in Africa to accelerate development of a stream of climate-smart varieties for small-scale producers, enabling their income growth, improved nutrition and national economic growth.
Dscription: Strengthening soybean crop improvement in Africa
Project Summary: This project seeks to modernize soybean breeding in sub-Saharan Africa by integrating genomic selection and speed breeding with traditional phenotypic methods. The primary objective is to accelerate the development and dissemination of high-yielding, stress-tolerant soybean varieties suitable for smallholder farmers. Key outputs include the establishment of robust breeding networks in East, Southern and West Africa and the alignment of breeding efforts with market intelligence and investment opportunities. This project will utilize Seed Equal tools and expertise, alongside existing and new seed company relationships, to ensure the effective delivery of new soybean varieties. These efforts aim to significantly improve soybean production and resilience in the region.
Project results: Establish a Speed Breeding Platform at IITA and implement Genomic Selection for higher genetic gain. Release rust-resistant varieties and accelerate seed multiplication with NARS partners. Integrate the 1000 Farms approach into IITA soybean breeding.

Name: R-A-2023-11-Specialty Rice
Dscription: SpecialtyRice
Project Summary: The major goal of this project is to develop a sustained system for specialty rice in Odisha for improved farm economics of growers and healthier rice choice for consumers.

Name: R-A-2023-46-SGP-GENOME EDITING Genome Editing Project
Dscription: SGP-GENOME EDITING Genome Editing Project
Project Summary: The Genome Editing Initiative (‘Genome Editing’) takes a holistic and long-term view of growing future demand for GEd solutions as experience with the technology and social acceptance grows. This initiative is a pilot effort to streamline the NARS-CGIAR gene editing innovation pipeline, and its supporting activities to reach the end user and create impacts.
Funder: Fundación para el Desarrollo Empresarial y Agrícola

Name: I0001 - SGP-BMI Genetic Innovation Transition Plan
Dscription: SGP-BMI Genetic Innovation Transition Plan
Project Summary: The EiB II: Cross-Crop Support Services for Breeding Acceleration project is a CIMMYT-led initiative funded by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (BMGF) to enhance breeding efficiency across CGIAR and National Agricultural Research and Extension Systems (NARES). The project focuses on accelerating the development and deployment of improved crop varieties by integrating cost-efficient shared breeding support services, rapid-cycle genomic selection, and digital breeding tools. These innovations aim to provide climate-resilient solutions for diverse cropping systems, ensuring that smallholder farmers have timely access to superior varieties. The project strengthens cross-institutional collaboration, enhances breeding data management, and improves scalability and efficiency within breeding pipelines. By optimizing genetic gain, standardizing breeding methodologies, and expanding partnerships, the initiative contributes to sustainable food security, climate adaptation, and increased productivity in developing regions.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: D0001-SGP-AVISA Accelerated varietal improvement and seed delivery of legumes and cereals in Africa
Dscription: SGP-AVISA Accelerated varietal improvement and seed delivery of legumes and cereals in Africa
Project Summary: The Accelerated Varietal Improvement and Seed Alliance (AVISA) – Transition Investment is a strategic initiative led by CIMMYT and funded by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (BMGF) to enhance breeding and seed delivery systems for key dryland cereal and legume crops in Sub-Saharan Africa. The project focuses on developing climate-resilient, high-yielding crop varieties that improve productivity and adaptability for small-scale farmers. Through collaborative breeding programs, technology transfer, and private sector engagement, AVISA supports the scaling of superior varieties while strengthening local seed systems to ensure long-term sustainability. The initiative also emphasizes gender inclusion, farmer capacity-building, and policy advocacy to facilitate the widespread adoption of improved crop varieties. By integrating cutting-edge research with on-the-ground implementation, AVISA aims to enhance food security, boost farmer resilience to climate change, and improve agricultural livelihoods in the region.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: R-A-2023-34-SGP-AGGRI2 AGGRI Project
Dscription: SGP-AGGRI2 AGGRI Project
Project Summary: AGGRi2 will support the implementation of the rapid cycle genomic selection (RCGS), a new breeding technology that uses mid-density genotyping and the genomic relationship matrix (GRM) to fundamentally redesign crop breeding pipelines. This will permit the acceleration of the breeding cycle from a current minimum of four years to two. Furthermore, it will allow much more extensive sampling of the target population of environments (TPE) during selection.
Countries: Mozambique, Philippines, Uganda, Egypt, Nigeria, India, Kenya, Ghana, Ethiopia, Senegal, Mali, Madagascar, Bangladesh

Name: A-AG10183-SGP-AGGRI2 Accelerating Genetic Gain and Varietal
Dscription: SGP-AGGRI2 Accelerating Genetic Gain and Varietal Replacement in Rice - Phase 2 Project
Project Summary: AGGRi2 is BMGF's investment in CGIAR's rice breeding and seed delivery work. This project builds on the accomplishments of previous BMGF projects such as Stress Tolerant Rice for Africa and South Asia (STRASA), Transforming Rice Breeding (TRB), and the AGGRi Alliance [Phase 1]. This second phase aims to develop and deliver improved rice varieties that respond to the needs of smallholder farmers in the SSA region. AGGRi2 is well aligned and linked to the CGIAR's Market Intelligence, Accelerated Breeding, and Seed Equal initiatives.
Project results: (1) Development and supply of improved rice varieties that meet the needs of small-scale farmers in the Sub-Saharan African region; (2) Replacement of older, lower-yielding varieties.

Name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation(BMGF)-1000 Farms Research Platform
Dscription: See project summary.
Project Summary: 1000FARMS aims to implement an Africa-wide network for on-farm variety testing that is digitally supported to provide vital performance data and farmer feedback. This will accelerate breeding of new climate-adapted and farmer-preferred crop varieties.  VISION: 1) CGIAR-NARES breeding networks have sufficient on-farm data on late-stage selection candidates and new releases to ensure they will be high-performing in the hands of farmers and can be recommended for scaling by the seed system; 2.) Farmers have sustainable access to new varieties with demonstratively improved performance over the varieties they currently grow across mandate crops in Africa and 3) Breeding programs deliver, sustain and accurately measure ‘realized’ or on-farm genetic gains in the face of climate change.
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: USA-BMGF-Artemis II - Field Phenotyping Prototype
Dscription: See project summary.
Project Summary: Artemis aims to transform breeding processes by leveraging AI and mobile phone technology to shift breeding programs from majority on-station to majority on-farm, alleviating critical logistical bottlenecks in phenotyping and shortening the breeding cycle. This is achieved through more efficient use of breeding technician time and provision of high quality and replicable data by enhancing the collection of reliable phenomic data through smartphones and to support breeder decision-making using on-farm collected data. A smartphone-deployable computer vision phenotyping tool makes phenotyping more efficient by reducing labor investment and overcoming limitations to the availability of highly trained technicians; reliability is increased as computer vision standardizes data collection and interpretation; and allows for the execution of on-farm breeding process as the phenotyping technology can be mobilized remotely. Computer vision leverages technology to collect data not possible by human eyes and it increases the amount, frequency and types of data, which can be used to derive novel breeding analytics expanding the data for decision making.
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: 1523-BMGF  RTB Breeding Investment
Dscription: RTB Breeding Investment
Project Summary:
In Africa, Root, Tuber, and Banana (RTB) crops (banana, cassava, potato, sweetpotato and yam) play a major role alleviating poverty, improving food security and climate resilience, and providing better livelihoods to farmers. Though plant breeding is a proven approach to develop varieties tailored to meet customer needs, to achieve success a relentless market-driven stance, constant modernization to increase genetic gains delivered on farmer’s fields, and links with seed systems to accelerate adoption of novel varieties are of essence. RTB Breeding, a BMGF investment focused on serving African farmers, aims to:
● Progressively streamline and mainstream modern breeding approaches in RTB crops, aiming to increase genetic gains delivered on farmer’s fields.
● Achieve progress towards developing effective and efficient CGIAR-NARS breeding networks in RTB crops.
● Quickly transition from a “one crop” mindset towards a shared one, fostering and incentivizing cross-crop, cross-center and cross-national programs collaborations to better serve farmers.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: D-200323-Root2Resilience: Root phenotyping and genetic improvement
Dscription: Root2Resilience: Root phenotyping and genetic improvement for rotational crops resilient to environmental change
Project Summary: The project aims to find strategies to boost crop breeding and connected research activities in their efforts to increase the resilience of cropping systems to climate change impacts through the development of innovative tools and the characterization of species, genotypes and molecular root traits associated with root and rhizosphere ideotypes that increase tolerance against abiotic stresses and carbon sequestration. To this end, the project hypothesize that crop adaptation to environmental constraints is mediated, at least in part, by the extended root phenotype, and that genotypic variation exists in prevalent and emerging crop species in root and rhizosphere traits that can be used to improve resilience of rotational systems to environmental change through breeding. The project utilizes a multidisciplinary research programme aimed at collecting, quantifying and deploying root traits in a range of agricultural-relevant situations for European and global agriculture.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2023-77 Rice Productivity MZ
Dscription: Rice Productivity in Mozambique
Project Summary:
The project aims to: (a) boost the production and distribution of high-yielding, climate-smart rice seed; (b) enhance crop productivity and market systems; and (c) build sustainable irrigation scheme management capacity. IRRI will focus on two key interventions in rice value chain development: (1) Strengthening the seed production system and (2) Enhancing the capacity of associations managing irrigation schemes.
 
 IRRI will partner with the Instituto de Investigação de Moçambique (IIAM) for EGS production and supply and collaborate with the National Institute of Irrigation (INIR) to provide technical support for irrigation management. Additionally, IRRI will collaborate with ADRA Mozambique, which will act as a market facilitator and deliver extension services via VBAs and SDAEs. ADRA, separately engaged by AGRA, will undertake complementary activities and deliverables.

Name: D-200271-Protecting Ethiopian Lentil Crops
Dscription: Protecting Ethiopian Lentil Crops
Project Summary:
The project aims to protect and increase the productivity of lentil crops in the mid-highlands of Ethiopia by delivering the solutions needed by smallholders growing lentils in the cereal-based cropping systems of Ethiopia such that they maintain and improve their current lentil cropping practices, their cereal crops, and their livelihood.
The project mobilizes the best expertise in Australia and ICARDA to support lentil breeding and pathology researchers at EIAR, provide germplasm with high level of resistance to target diseases, establish sustainable disease management, thereby restoring resilience of a farming system under threat from diseases of lentil. The project delivers to smallholders growing lentils in the cereal-based cropping systems of Ethiopia the solutions needed to maintain and improve their current lentil cropping practices, their cereal crops, and their livelihood. These achievements will be delivered through research focused on testing management options and new lentil varieties.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: T-PJ-003651-Program for Seed System Innovation for VPCs in Africa (PROSSIVA)
Dscription: Program for Seed System Innovation for VPCs in Africa (PROSSIVA)
Project Summary: The Africa Centre of Innovation for Vegetatively Propagated Crop (VPC) Seed Systems (ACI-VSS) aims to catalyze the development, validation and scaling of innovative packages to establish functional and profitable seed systems in target African countries. By 2030, it seeks to positively impact tens of millions of seed entrepreneurs, smallholder farmers and consumers, contributing to poverty reduction, improved livelihoods and new job opportunities, especially for women and youth. Operating under the OneCGIAR Initiative ‘SeEdQUAL: Delivering Genetic Gains in Farmers’ Fields’, ACI-VSS will develop and deploy technological, marketing and institutional innovations to address seed delivery bottlenecks for major VPCs. Its first phase (2022-2026) will focus on eight target countries, aiming to sustain operations through a diverse membership platform.
Project results: Functional Seed Systems for banana, cassava, potato, sweet potato and yam in the target countries of Sub-saharan Africa.

Name: R-A-2019-137-PPP Ph2
Dscription: PPP Ph2
Project Summary: The Platform would provide training, technical services, technical assistance, and germplasm to private sector companies interested in developing/establishing their in-house commercial inbred and hybrid rice breeding capacity. To address the shortfall in the development of market-driven superior rice varieties and the production of quality seeds, IRRI will employ modernized cutting-edge breeding techniques coupled with the rapid deployment of new rice varieties. Partnering private companies will get customized services from IRRI, which include (i) human resources development through group short course training sessions at IRRI’s basic breeding lab, molecular lab, grain quality lab, statistical and biometrical data analysis, and hands-on training at demonstration trial sites, (ii) technical services to develop or improve rice breeding capacity (human, physical, and institutional), and (iii) access to rice germplasm/lines.

Name: Redesigning banana breeding
Dscription: PJ-003876: Redesigning banana breeding

Name: T-PJ-003778-SGP-RTB - Roots Tubers and Bananas Breeding: a consolidated investment
Dscription: PJ-003778: SGP-RTB - Roots Tubers and Bananas Breeding: a consolidated investment
Project Summary: This RTB Breeding Project aims to alleviate poverty and enhance food and nutrition security in sub-Saharan Africa by developing market-preferred, gender-sensitive and climate-resilient varieties of RTB crops. Key objectives include implementing modern breeding approaches, optimizing breeding networks and fostering cross-crop collaboration in Ghana, Mozambique, Nigeria, Tanzania and Uganda. Expected outcomes involve advancing breeding techniques, consolidating state-of-the-art practices and developing technologies essential for effective breeding. This project targets smallholder farmers, processors and consumers, with a focus on improving their quality of life and food security while promoting gender equality and environmental sustainability. The goal is to build a resilient and inclusive agricultural system across diverse regions.
Project results: Breeding programs remain market-driven, leveraging genomic predictions, advanced statistical designs, and high-density testing. Population and product development are decoupled, with stringent QA/QC ensuring genetic gains. A breeding network model is piloted, supported by biometrics, bioinformatics, and breeding databases. Effective management fosters talent development, knowledge sharing, and operational excellence.
Countries: Uganda, Sierra Leone, Mozambique, South Africa, Rwanda, Côte d'Ivoire, Kenya, Congo, Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, Zambia

Name: T-PJ-003776: Crops to End Hunger (CteH) Research project "CtEH-07: Upgrading Infrastructure and Facilities for Accelerated Breeding and Genetic Gain in Cassava; CteH-16 Regional Germplasm Hub for Vegetatively Propagated Crops @ KEPHIS Muguga; CtEH-OneCGIAR-Facilities upgrade and Mechanization Improvement"
Dscription: PJ-003776: Crops to End Hunger (CteH) Research project "CtEH-07: Upgrading Infrastructure and Facilities for Accelerated Breeding and Genetic Gain in Cassava; CteH-16 Regional Germplasm Hub for Vegetatively Propagated Crops @ KEPHIS Muguga; CtEH-OneCGIAR-Facilities upgrade and Mechanization Improvement"

Name: M0369-1000Farms-MaizeSelect
Dscription: 1000Farms-MaizeSelect
Project Summary: The purpose is to pilot new genomics-driven breeding methods that select varieties directly on the farms of African small-scale producers, ensuring that new varieties are adapted to their management and the rapidly changing climate.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo
Countries: South Africa, Mozambique, Zimbabwe, Zambia, Malawi

Name: N-644006 - ACCELERATING CROP IMPROVEMENT THROUGH GENOME EDITING
Dscription: ACCELERATING CROP IMPROVEMENT THROUGH GENOME EDITING
Project Summary: To support CGIAR genome editing research using a product life cycle approach

Name: D-200319-Accelerating genetic gain in wheat through hybrid breeding
Dscription: Accelerating genetic gain in wheat through hybrid breeding in Bangladesh, Ethiopia and Pakistan
Project Summary:
This project proposes to develop a novel, cost effective and practical system to rapidly produce large numbers of wheat hybrid combinations for testing in breeding programs, and to produce at an acceptable cost, large amounts of hybrid seed for sale to farmers. The project aims to extend the benefits of hybrid wheat to researchers, wheat breeders, farmers and consumers in Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Ethiopia by establishing a hybrid wheat seed industry in each country. 
The project will establish and activate the value chain for wheat hybrids in each country working on evaluation of farmer interest for wheat hybrids locally, exploring heterosis locally and identification of key parental materials, scaling up the local production of wheat hybrids and maintenance of the system and engaging with local seed suppliers to explore impediments to establishing a viable hybrid seed industry. The feasibility of introducing hybrid wheat, including assessment of hybrid high performance (thereby providing motivation) and seed affordability (thereby providing accessibility), will be established in Pakistan, Ethiopia and Bangladesh. If successful, the project would establish new wheat seed industries in all three countries. However, the impact could extend globally using all four countries (Australia included) as models.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R0218-Accelerating Genetic Gains in Maize & Wheat (DFID-DF
Dscription: Accelerating Genetic Gains in Maize & Wheat (DFID-DF)
Project Summary: Accelerating Genetic Gains in Maize & Wheat aims to accelerate the development and delivery of more productive, climate-resilient, market-demanded, and nutritious maize and wheat varieties in support of sustainable agricultural transformation in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: D-100838-GRDC Genetic Diversity
Dscription: Achieving improved genetic gain for yield in chickpea, faba bean and lentil using genetic diversity
Project Summary: The project presents a comprehensive four-year partnership with Morduch University in Australia. The project aims to broaden the genetic diversity of chick pea, faba bean and lentil crops, identify superior genotypes, and develop a Genomic Selection (GS) model to enhance breeding programs. The objectives include sharing germplasm with Australian partners, seed multiplication, phenotyping and evaluation, field trials, genotyping, and data analysis. The outcomes include expanded genetic diversity, identification of superior genotypes, enhanced breeding programs, publications for knowledge dissemination, and capacity building for young researchers. This project represents a significant step towards improving the genetic potential and resilience of these crops, contributing to global food security in the face of climate change and growing demand.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: PJ-003953: APPUI A LA TRANSFORMATION ET A LA RESILIENCE DES SYSTEMES ALIMENTAIRES DE MADAGASCAR A TRAVERS LA RECHERCHE POUR LE DEVELOPPEMENT
Dscription: AfricaRice/IITA- APPUI A LA TRANSFORMATION ET A LA RESILIENCE DES SYSTEMES ALIMENTAIRES DE MADAGASCAR A TRAVERS LA RECHERCHE POUR LE DEVELOPPEMENT
Project results: The project focused on strengthening research and agricultural development through capacity building, infrastructure enhancement, and improved seed systems. Key outputs included research center evaluations, technical training for scientists and staff, development of variety registration guides, and studies on production systems. It also established demonstration sites, introduced climate-smart agricultural equipment, improved seed and livestock varieties, and supported seed enterprises. Additionally, it promoted disease management and agronomic best practices among farmers.

Name: R-A-2021-76-Allele mining
Dscription: Allele mining
Project Summary: The project will support faster and more cost-effective discovery and deployment of climate-adaptive alleles from the world’s germplasm collections. Advances in genotyping, phenotyping, climate and soil data availability, and novel analysis approaches, combined with improved management of breeding in One CGIAR, have converged to provide a pathway towards systematic mobilization of the world’s genetic resources in support of climate change adaptation. This project will pilot these integrated approaches for five of our focus crops (cassava, maize, sorghum cowpea, and rice), providing a scalable model for the rapid and cost-effective discovery and deployment of alleles that can improve the climate resilience of the crops on which African and South Asian farmers and consumers depend for food and income security. The project will support the entire global effort to deliver more productive and stress-tolerant varieties to SSPs, supporting the adaptation of cropping systems in SA and SSA to rapid climate change.

Name: L-CIA024-Anti-methanogenic feedstock for livestock systems in Global South
Dscription: Anti-methanogenic feedstock for livestock systems in Global South
Project Summary:
This project aims to discover and deploy high-yielding, tropically adapted forages that reduce cattle methane emissions and improve productivity for small-scale livestock farmers and pastoralists. The focus areas are:

Identifying and promoting AMC-rich forage legumes in ruminant production systems in the Global South through partnerships, including new public-private partnerships (PPP).
Breeding methane-reducing traits into widely used forage-grass cultivars using conventional or precision-breeding approaches.
Enabling the deployment of AMC-rich forages into livestock systems, including cattle grazing and cut-and-carry smallholder systems, through scaling and impact investments.
The project leverages a partnership between science, philanthropy, and public and private sector partners to develop and deploy AMC-enriched forages, reducing methane production in cattle while maintaining or improving productivity. This strategy offers a cost-effective solution for reducing livestock emissions in the Global South.

Name: A-AG10171-ARISE PDRCC
Dscription: ARISE PDRCC
Project Summary: The African Research Initiative for Scientific Excellence (ARISE) project is designed to advance scientific research and innovation across Africa by supporting early-career researchers in addressing critical challenges on the continent. ARISE provides funding, mentorship, and resources to enable these researchers to pursue cutting-edge projects that contribute to the continent's development. The initiative emphasizes the importance of building local research capacities, fostering collaboration among African scientists, and promoting research that is relevant to the needs of African societies. Through ARISE, researchers are empowered to make significant contributions to fields such as agriculture, health, environment, and technology, ultimately driving progress and enhancing the continent's scientific and economic landscape.
Project results: (1) Acquisition of new knowledge on rice-rice blast interaction, (2) development of new resistant breeding lines, (3) capacity building and (4) broadening collaboration - all of which will accelerate rice breeding and ensure the sustainability of rice production in sub-Saharan Africa in the face of climate change

Name: D-200388-Back to the future, from landraces to high-yielding, heat an
Dscription: Back to the future, from landraces to high-yielding, heat and drought-tolerant barley preferred by East African farmers
Project Summary:
The project aims to deliver high-yielding, climate change resilient, fit-for-purpose Ethiopian barley, meeting the demands of both men and women farmers. For this purpose, state-of-the-art methods are used to optimize genomic predictions coupled with multi-environmental trials in Morocco and Ethiopia and gender and age-based participatory selection. The project screens a panel of elite germplasm developed from crosses between Ethiopian landraces and elite genotypes and cultivars from ICARDA Global Barley Breeding Program of the CGIAR, at two sites at the CGIAR Heat and Drought precision phenotyping platform of the CGIAR in Morocco and four in Ethiopia for two years to identify heat and drought tolerant sources as well as drudgery reduction, market and other wellbeing traits. 
The project researches’ perform association genetic analysis (GWAS) with the aim of identifying QTL associated with yield under drought and heat conditions as well as specific adaptation to Ethiopian conditions. In addition, the project develops advanced molecular breeding techniques such as the use of genomic estimated breeding values (GEBV) to predict performance and guide selections, and disseminate the results of this research via scientific articles in high-impact factor journals.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: D-200314-BarleyMicroBreed: Strategies for breeding climate c
Dscription: BarleyMicroBreed: Strategies for breeding climate change resilient barley, genetically equipped to optimized root-microbiome interactions
Project Summary:
BarleyMicroBreed builds on the paradigm that crop resource efficiency and stress resilience can be significantly improved by optimizing the capacity of plant roots to efficiently interact with the existing soil microbiota. Therefore, the project proposes to advance mechanistic understanding of interactions between the crop plant genome, root phenotypic traits, and the root-associated microbiota to identify novel breeding strategies for crops tailored to harness the benefits of the indigenous soil microbial diversity. 
The overall objective of BarleyMicroBreed is to establish links between barley genome components and root microbiomes that facilitate root traits pivotal for drought stress resilience, nutrient efficiency and belowground carbon restitution. Based on this, the project will identify strategies for breeding crops optimized to harness stress resilience from soil biodiversity, thereby reducing the need for external inputs. Breeding for crops tailored to harness the benefits of the indigenous soil microbial diversity rather than inoculating crops with plant-beneficial microorganisms will be a much more feasible and long-lasting strategy.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: Bihar Centre Of Excellence For Millets Value Chain (BCoEMVC)
Dscription: Bihar Centre Of Excellence For Millets Value Chain (BCoEMVC)
Project Summary:
ICRISAT, in collaboration with the Government of Bihar, will establish a “Centre of Excellence for Millets and Value Chain (CoE-MVC)”, in collaboration with the Government of Bihar will establish a “Centre of Excellence for Millets and Value Chain (CoE-MVC)”, to promote research, breeding, production, mechanization, and value chain as a long-term vision for sustainable promotion of millets to diversify its crop and food systems. Overall, this concept aims to enhance Bihar’s crop and food systems by addressing the challenges of Bihar state with respect to nutritional deficiencies, food insecurity, climate risk and low farm income through crop diversification and value chain development activities. 
As part of the project, ICRISAT will conduct a techno-economic feasibility study and develop Ready-to-Cook (RTC) and Ready-to-Eat (RTE) millet products, which will be integrated into the state's mid-day meal program.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: N-300044-Biotechnology Innovations
Dscription: Biotechnology Innovations
Project Summary: This project supports a package of technical assistance and IFPRI analytical capacities to support an enabling biosafety platform for Genetically Engineered and Genome Editing technologies with applications for agriculture development. The Concept Note answers a call for a competitive bid to USAID’s Agriculture Biotechnology for Global Food Security Annual Program Statement related to specifically to the policy enabling climate. It is a follow on to previous USAID funding to the Program for Biosafety systems but includes an expanded suite of IFPRI analytic approaches and new partners to address new regulatory challenges and the evolving political economy surrounding these technologies. It may also include new geographies and addresses a more focused effort with CG centers on biosafety. It is a 5 year project.

Name: A-AG10181-BMGF MultiHarvestRice
Dscription: BMGF MultiHarvestRice
Project Summary: The MultiHarvestRice project, led by AfricaRice, is a transformative initiative aimed at enhancing rice production across Africa through the introduction of high-yielding and climate-resilient rice varieties. The project addresses the critical need for increased rice productivity and sustainability in the face of growing environmental challenges and food security concerns. A central component of the project is the development and promotion of advanced rice varieties that are not only high-yielding but also resilient to climate-related stresses such as droughts, floods, and heat. These new varieties are designed to thrive in diverse environmental conditions, thereby reducing the risks associated with climate variability and improving overall crop stability. By providing farmers with access to these improved varieties, the project helps to boost rice yields and ensure more reliable production.
Project results: A-AG10181-BMGF MultiHarvestRice

Name: T-PJ-003994-BMGF/IITA - YAM OPTIMIZED ARCHITECTURE THROUGH G
Dscription: BMGF/IITA - YAM OPTIMIZED ARCHITECTURE THROUGH GENE EDITING (YOAGA) W3 (GI) - PJ-3994...L.TRIPATHI
Project results: Target genes for yam’s growth habit are identified for editing to develop semi-dwarf varieties. Gene editing constructs and tools like FULCRUM are available. Transformation efficiency improves, and gene-edited yam is developed and evaluated in greenhouses. Edited events integrate into IITA’s breeding program, with labor requirement insights for Nigeria.

Name: CANADA -GAC - Beans For Women For Empowerment  In Eastern DRC (BEANS4WOMEN)
Dscription: Both description and project summary both filled. The "Beans for Women for Empowerment (Beans4Women)" project aims to strengthen gender equality, participation in decision-making, and resource rights of women and girls active in the bean sector in three conflict-prone provinces of Eastern DRC: North Kivu, South Kivu, and Tanganyika. The project addresses social inequalities, poverty, and gender-based violence while leveraging the significant participation of women in the bean economy to drive transformative change. Scope and Activities are: 1) Business Environment: Upskilling and brokering new business opportunities for women-led bean enterprises and female entrepreneurs + Accelerating the uptake of climate-smart technologies by women bean producers; 2) Leadership and Decision-Making: Empowering women and girl voices in bean economy leadership and decision-making + trengthening the capacity and responsiveness of the public sector to deliver gender-responsive programming and policies; 3) Resource Rights: Improving and enforcing institutional arrangements around women’s land rights + Outreach, education, and bean product development activities aimed at influencing nutrition behaviors and attitudes, making fortified, improved, or high-iron beans a bigger part of diets in Eastern DRC. Implementation and Partnerships are: 1) Building on robust existing partnerships with local and international NGOs and bean value chain actors; 2) Engaging with local partners to co-develop activities that generate sustainable and gender-sensitive outputs; 3) Monitoring and Evaluation: Establishing a conflict sensitivity action plan + Regular reporting on progress and achievements, linked directly to the performance measurement framework.
Project Summary: The "Beans for Women for Empowerment (Beans4Women)" project in Eastern DRC focuses on enhancing gender equality, decision-making, and resource rights for women and girls in the bean sector. Targeting North Kivu, South Kivu, and Tanganyika provinces, the project aims to upskill women-led enterprises, promote climate-smart agricultural practices, and empower women in leadership roles. Major outcomes include increased business opportunities, improved climate resilience, and strengthened land rights. The project is expected to directly benefit 2 million people, primarily women and girls, and indirectly reach 10 million people over five years by leveraging robust partnerships and inclusive, sustainable interventions.
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)
Countries: Burundi, Ghana, Zimbabwe, Uganda, Ethiopia, Malawi, Madagascar, Rwanda, Cameroon, Mozambique, Kenya, Zambia, Guinea

Name: D-200359-Breeding European Legumes for Increased Sustainability
Dscription: Breeding European Legumes for Increased Sustainability - BELIS
Project Summary: The Breeding European Legumes for Increased Sustainability (BELIS) aims to increase the competitiveness of the EU and Associated Countries legume breeding industry by improving the breeding methodologies and the governance structures of the sector, while designing conditions that allow an efficient delivery of the genetic progress achieved by the breeders to the other actors in the value chain (seed industry, registration offices, extension services, farmers, feed and food industry). The project focuses on seven forage crops (lucerne, red clover, white clover, annual clovers, sainfoin, birdsfoot trefoil and vetches) and seven grain crops (pea, faba bean, soybean, white lupin, lentil, chickpea, common bean) that are currently grown to produce feed (for ruminants – cattle, sheep, goat – and monogastric animals – pig, poultry), food (as is or after processing) or to deliver ecosystem services (here called “service crops”). This list includes underutilised species with revived for novel cropping systems and markets. The project develops tools and methodologies for cost-effective legume breeding programmes and deliver proofs of concepts, with and for breeders. Additionally, the project facilitates an economic and regulatory environment that activates legume breeding. And finally, the project implements an efficient, ambitious and durable transfer of knowledge and innovation among researchers, plant breeders and other actors through the establishment of a platform dedicated to legume breeding.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2023-25-C4 Offshoots
Dscription: C4 Offshoots
Project Summary: The five-year program will be divided into three workstreams (WS), two of which will have go/no-go decision points at the end of year 3. In the first phase of WS1 & WS3 (years 1-3), previously observed transgenic results will be validated and methods to activate endogenous gene expression by gene editing will be developed. Phase 2 of these workstreams (years 4-5) will only be initiated if the transgenic lines deliver enhanced yield/resilience in field trials across three seasons, and if proof of concept for overexpression through gene editing has been demonstrated. Yield in this context is bulk grain yield per plot in the target environment and resilience is the grain yield of the target population in managed irrigation conditions compared to yield in simulated stress conditions. In Phase 2, gene edited lines will be generated that genocopy the transgenic lines and performance will be evaluated in the field for at least one season.

Name: L-UOE012-Centre for Tropical Livestock Genetics and Health -
Dscription: Centre for Tropical Livestock Genetics and Health - Phase 2 (FCDO-DF)
Project Summary:
The Centre for Tropical Livestock Genetics and Health (CTLGH) is a collaborative initiative between the International Livestock Research Institute (ILRI) and the University of Edinburgh. It focuses on improving the productivity and resilience of livestock in tropical environments through advanced genetics and genomics. The project aims to develop livestock breeds that are better adapted to tropical climates, more resistant to diseases, and more productive, thereby supporting the livelihoods of small-scale farmers and pastoralists in the Global South.

CTLGH works on identifying genetic traits that enhance livestock performance and resilience, leveraging cutting-edge technologies such as gene editing and genomic selection. The project also emphasizes capacity building and knowledge transfer, working closely with local and international partners to ensure the practical application of research findings. By integrating science with development goals, CTLGH seeks to enhance food security, economic stability, and environmental sustainability in tropical regions.

Name: T-PJ-003911-CGIAR Genetic Innovations Public-Private Partnership Platform
Dscription: CGIAR Genetic Innovations Public-Private Partnership Platform
Project Summary: The Genetic Innovations-Public Private Partnership (GI-PPP) platform aims to advance the modernization of Genetic Innovations (GI) by facilitating collaborations with private sector companies. The platform's main objective is to streamline the engagement process between these companies and CGIAR breeding programs, reducing transaction costs through a specialized unit adept in forming and managing partnerships. By creating at least five robust public-private partnerships, the GI-PPP intends to significantly enhance the effectiveness of GI breeding programs. These partnerships are expected to bring substantial in-kind contributions from the private sector, which will help offset modernization costs and accelerate the adoption of innovative breeding technologies.
Project results: Develop five public-private partnerships, securing private sector funding and in-kind investments for CG crop breeding. Implement industry best practices across Genetic Innovation (GI) breeding programs and integrate the GI public-private partnership platform into core Genetic Innovation programming.

Name: Developing and introducing high-yielding climate-resilient pigeon pea cultivars for intensification and diversification of cropping systems and to satisfy the nutritional requirement of small holder farmers of Odisha
Dscription: Developing and introducing high-yielding climate-resilient pigeon pea cultivars for intensification and diversification of cropping systems and to satisfy the nutritional requirement of small holder farmers of Odisha
Project Summary:
To promote high-yielding climate-resilient pigeonpea cultivars for intensification of cropping systems and to meet the nutritional and other livelihood needs of small-holder farmers in Odisha
1. Strengthen the pigeonpea breeding program of the Centre for Pulses Research (CPR), Berhampur and RRTTS, Bhawanipatna, from OUAT by developing a strong pigeonpea breeding pipeline.
2. Modernize pigeonpea breeding operations of Centre for Pulses Research (CPR), Berhampur, and RRTTS, Bhawanipatna from OUAT
3. Identify, evaluate and release high-yielding, fusarium wilt and sterility mosaic-resistant varieties and hybrids of pigeonpea in early and mid-early duration and well adapted to cropping systems of the target districts
4. Evaluate the potential of newly developed photo-insensitive super-early pigeonpea varieties with market-preferred traits in rice fallows
5. Promote the adoption of farmer and market-preferred varieties and hybrids of pigeonpea with Improved Pigeonpea Production Technologies (IPPT) through citizen science approach “triadic comparisons of technologies(tricot).”
6. To build the capacity of farmers and Farmer Producers Organizations (FPOs) in seed production of farmer-preferred improved varieties for improving the availability of genetically pure seed.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: P-1568-WBS0-Developing Local Seed Potato Production System i
Dscription: Developing Local Seed Potato Production System in West Bengal
Project Summary: The ARC Project in West Bengal aims to tackle the critical issue of the unavailability of quality potato seeds of popular varieties, which leads to low productivity and poor tuber quality in the state. To address this, the project plans to enhance seed and varietal replacement and improve tuber quality by developing strategies to supply clean, healthy planting material of new and existing varieties preferred by all value chain actors, including the processing industry, using the low-cost apical rooted cutting (ARC) technology. This strategy involves establishing new infrastructure and efficiently utilizing existing facilities to expand the production of tissue culture plantlets, apical rooted cuttings, G0 production, and further multiplication.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: R-A-2022-52-Direct Seeding Indo Gangetic
Dscription: Direct Seeding Indo Gangetic
Project Summary: The project will introduce a planned deployment of haplotypes essential to high performance under reduced-irrigation Dry Direct Seeding. This is achieved in parallel to quantitative breeding efforts through IRRI´s centralized, demand-driven trait development pipeline, which is designed to deliver robust, large-effect alleles needed for specific market segments/TPEs in a form that breeders can easily incorporate without the penalties usually associated with introducing new genetics to elite material.

Name: R-A-2023-19-EBS Phase 3
Dscription: EBS Phase 3
Project Summary: The project supports the deployment of the Enterprise Breeding System (EBS) -  an effective data management system that allows the integration of genotypic data into complex statistical analyses to drastically increase the data accuracy at a fraction of the cost of more traditional methods. Through this, breeding networks serving small-scale producers (SSPs) throughout the developing areas worldwide will have access to genomic selections, and drive the positive transformation of breeding networks.
Countries: Mexico, Nigeria, Senegal, Philippines, Kenya, Morocco
Funder: Fundación para el Desarrollo Empresarial y Agrícola

Name: D-200261-Egypt Bilateral Program 2020-2021 and 2021-2022
Dscription: Egypt Bilateral Program 2020-2021 and 2021-2022 Seasons
Project Summary:
The project continues to build on an ever-growing research partnership in Egypt to improving livelihoods and enhancing food security.  The Bilaterla program works on realizing the following objectives: 
1)	Screening of new germplasm in wheat: In order to make the germplasm resistant to dust, result in high yields, and improve their resistance to heat;
2)	Introducing new germplasm on barley to select the best genotypes for breadmaking and animal feeding. 
3)	Improving screening and selection of new germplasm on faba beans and other legumes to result in high yield faba bean. 
4)	Promote water saving innovations for improving water productivity, and achieve precision irrigation in old and new reclaimed lands in Egypt.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: D-200358-Enhancing Food Security and Climate Resilience in Morocco and Tunisia
Dscription: Enhancing Food Security and Climate Resilience in Morocco and Tunisia
Project Summary:
The project will be carried out through partnership of ICARDA, INRA-Morocco and INRAT-Tunisia by involving all key stake holders targeting the rainfed wheat based production systems of both countries. The project is designed with the following main objectives: 
(1) Rapid development of high yielding varieties with resistance/tolerance to drought, heat and diseases through innovative approaches  
(2) Asses the performance of the existing seed systems, and design, test and establish more efficient and gender responsive seed systems 
(3) Multiplication of existing improved varieties (recently released but not adopted) 
(4) Establish context-specific and gender-responsive innovation platforms to pilot and demonstrate the impact of improved varieties and crop management practices on productivity, income and climate resilience 
(5) capacity development and knowledge translation for impact at scale. 
The rapid variety development scheme using joint germplasm selection and participatory variety selection approach at key locations will help to shorten the time required to develop and release a variety from 10 years to 4 years.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: Enhancing groundnut productivity through increased quality seed access in Odisha
Dscription: Enhancing groundnut productivity through increased quality seed access in Odisha
Project Summary:
The overall objective of the project is to increase access to quality groundnut seed for cultivation in the state.
Specific goals:
1. Train the farmers in groundnut seed production.
2. Increase the production and supply of quality groundnut seed.
3. Replace old and obsolete varieties with new improved groundnut varieties.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: PJ-003978: Cassava Source Sink (CASS) Phase III
Dscription: FAU/IITA- Cassava Source Sink (CASS) Phase III- PJ-3978
Project Summary: This third phase of the Cassava Source Sink Project aims to significantly enhance cassava yield and quality on smallholder farms in Sub-Saharan Africa by optimizing plant physiology and source-sink relationships. This project's objectives are to develop nutrient-efficient genotypes with improved sink capacity, modify the shoot-root ratio and enhance traits like photosynthesis and starch accumulation through genetic engineering and breeding. The expected outputs of the project include high-yield, nutrient-rich cassava plants and a comprehensive database (CASSbase) for future research. This project targets increased cassava production, which will boost food security and economic stability for smallholder farmers and benefit agricultural researchers and local economies.
Project results: Develop high-yield, nutritionally enhanced cassava genotypes through genetic and metabolic engineering. Increase smallholder cassava production for food security and economic stability. Establish CASSbase, a comprehensive database for research and breeding initiatives.

Name: R-A-2024-145-Healthier Rice
Dscription: Healthier Rice
Project Summary: This is a co-funding by USAID in support of the main BMGF investment that aims to obtain regulatory approvals and see the deployment of Golden Rice not only in Bangladesh and the Philippines but as well in Indonesia. obtain regulatory approvals for high iron and zinc rice (HIZR), and initiate the stacking of Golden Rice and HIZR traits in important rice varieties.

Name: N-644H03-HP–GAC–ENFS
Dscription: HP–GAC–ENFS
Project Summary: Expanding the reach of nutrient-enriched staples for sustainable food systems transformation with the goal of improved access to and control of nutritious climte-smart seed, food, and knowledge, for vulnerable communities, especially women and girls, in Bangladesh, Pakistan, India, Uganda, and Zimbabwe.

Name: R-A-2012-35 HRDC
Dscription: Hybrid Rice Development Consortium
Project Summary: A multi-sectoral consortium on hybrid rice research, production of hybrid rice varieties, promotion of innovations - including access to new germplasm, and information on hybrid rice technology.
Countries: Belgium, India, Benin, China, Kenya, Mali, Germany, Nepal, Egypt, Bangladesh, Colombia, Pakistan, Indonesia, Malaysia, Sri Lanka, The Republic of Korea, Myanmar, Singapore, Philippines, Thailand, Dominican Republic, Costa Rica
Funder: Fundación para el Desarrollo Empresarial y Agrícola

Name: R-A-2022-71-ICAR 2023 - 2027
Project Summary: The project has 5 thematic work areas, namely: 1) Establish a framework for developing market oriented rice varieties with higher genetic gains using IRRI’s OneRice Breeding Strategy; 2) Integrate direct seeded rice system for India by combining the strengths of DSR-breeding with best agronomic practices, with special emphasis on the Eastern India; 3) Rice-based Health and Nutrition through improved starch quality to lower the glycemic index (GI) and increased nutrient density in the milled rice through breeding interventions; 4) Climate Resilient Rice through the development of agronomic practices that help to mitigate the effects of rice cultivation on the climate, specifically in terms of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.; 5) Integrated Capacity Building.

Name: Increasing Pulses Productivity and Incomes through Science-led Interventions in rice fallows of Odisha
Dscription: Increasing Pulses Productivity and Incomes through Science-led Interventions in rice fallows of Odisha
Project Summary:
The overall goal of the mission is to establish exemplar sites of learning demonstrating increased average productivity of agricultural systems in the selected districts of Odisha. The specific objectives are as follows:
1. On-farm participatory evaluation of an early maturing variety of chickpea, pigeonpea and green gram in target districts.
2. Enhancing rice fallows' crop productivity and income by adopting the best bet agronomic practices such as zero tillage and soil test-based micronutrient application.
3. To build the capacity of the farmers and stakeholders in the sustainable management of rice fallows in identified districts of Odisha.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: D-200347-India Collaborative Program 2022/2023 to 2026/2027
Dscription: India Collaborative Program 2022/2023 to 2026/2027
Project Summary: The India Collaborative Program between the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR), the university of Agriculture Sciences -Dharwad and ICARDA “Raising Genetic Gain of Indian Dryland Durum Wheat” is set under the development of farmer-preferred and climate-resilient barley and durum wheat varieties with higher and stable yield for diverse agro-ecologies in India. The project aims at developing varieties matching the EiB developed product profiles by conducting joint multi-location trails in Central zone, peninsular zone and other selected locations. The project will aim at having at least 40 crosses made to combine ICARDA and Indian Germplasm. In addition, the project supports the food industry with high quality, local and and affordable gains, and local communities with highly nutritious gains.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: D-200211-Intelligent Collections of Food Legumes Genetic Res
Dscription: Intelligent Collections of Food Legumes Genetic Resources for European Agrofood Systems (INCREASE)
Project Summary:
The objective of INCREASE is to improve the sustainable use of plant genetic resources in food and agriculture (GenRes) by enhancing, in food legumes, the genotypic and phenotypic characterization of GenRes and by developing efficient and effective conservation tools and methods in order to promote agrobiodiversity and its use in Europe. INCREASE will enhance the status of the GenRes of four important food legumes (chickpea, common bean, lentil, lupin), and promote their conservation and use. This will contribute to development of methodologies and tools that can be applied to, and should benefit, all kinds of GenRes. 
ICARDA’s role in the project is to conserve genetic diversity and its use by bringing novel genes into cultivars, develop improved and adapted crops and livestock for current and emerging biotic and abiotic constraints, build resilient integrated crop-livestock farming systems, develop efficient value chains with supportive policies and viable off-farm activities, and to reach sustainable use and management of scarce water and land resources, and finally, to develop the capacity for the delivery of quality research and sustainable development impact with gender equity and youth participation. 
ICARDA will contribute into capturing and characterizing genetic diversity in lentil and chickpea germplasm and revealing novel information to users. ICARDA will also contribute in constitution of reference-core germplasm set, field phenotyping of training core germplasm under the WP4 besides indirect involvement in other work packages.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: A-AG10140-KAFACI Phase 3
Dscription: KAFACI Phase 3
Project Summary: The KAFACI (Korean-African Food and Agriculture Cooperation Initiative) project, led by AfricaRice, is a transformative effort aimed at revolutionizing rice production across Africa. The initiative addresses the continent’s rice production challenges through a comprehensive strategy. It focuses on developing and disseminating high-yield, disease-resistant rice varieties, promoting advanced farming technologies, and implementing best practices in water and nutrient management. The project collaborates closely with local farmers, agricultural researchers, and government agencies to ensure that the improvements are both practical and sustainable. A significant aspect of KAFACI is its emphasis on capacity-building. The project includes training programs and knowledge exchange activities designed to empower local stakeholders, enhancing their ability to implement new technologies and practices effectively.
Project results: (1) Development of High-Yielding Rice Varieties; (2) Establishment of the Africa-Korea Rice Breeding Laboratory; (2) Yields, income  and food consumption scores increased

Name: R-A-2021-28-KOICA-UPLB Project
Dscription: KOICA-UPLB Project
Project Summary: The project is a partnership between IRRI, the Korean Internatiol Cooperation Agency and the University of the Philippines, Los Banos and is aimed at establishing a state-of-the-art Genome research center, enhancing the R&D capacity through training, education and scientific exchange between the university and IRRI in the area of genomic research.

Name: D-200344-Lentil and Chickpea Development in teh Mediterranea
Dscription: Lentil and Chickpea Development in the Mediterranean Area
Project Summary: ICARDA and TERRES INOVIA, in partnership with French Breeders work through this project to widen the genetic base of French germplasm for lentil and chickpea. Exploitation of new and diverse sources of variation is needed for the genetic enhancement of grain legumes. Wild relatives and landraces with enhanced levels of resistance/tolerance to multiple stresses provide important sources of genetic diversity for crop improvement. The project aims to bring in elite material from ICARDA on the one hand and material in segregation from pre-breeding work on the other hand. All the material are assessed in France in the main production areas of these two species, in order to identify relevant material to improve and diversify the French germplasm for these two species. In return for access to the material developed and provided by ICARDA, the breeding companies will contribute to the Project through the material assessment network. Finally, the developed fixed material present in the genetic pool will be shared for multi-location and multiannual trials in France to assess their agronomic value and characterize them in various environment on an annual basis.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2024-25-Madhya Pradesh_Rice Inbreds and Hybrids
Dscription: Madhya Pradesh_Rice Inbreds and Hybrids
Project Summary: The project aims to (1) develop an annual plan and strategy for sustainable and low carbon rice transformation in target countries; (2) develop training materials on scale-appropriate sustainable and low carbon rice (including hands-on manual, e-learning, and videos); 3) enhance knowledge and adoption of sustainable and low carbon rice through training, field demonstrations, and technology transfers.

Name: R-A-2023-67-MHRA
Dscription: MHRA
Project Summary: The project aims to identify and evaluate rice varieties and management practices to promote multiple-harvest rice production. This system offers a low-cost, labor-saving intensification approach for rice-based cropping systems in Sub-Saharan Africa. It focuses on adapting rice varieties and management strategies to increase profitability, reduce production costs, and support climate change adaptation in African rice systems. By utilizing ratooning, the project seeks to maximize rice yield and reduce labor inputs, benefiting small-scale producers, especially women farmers.
Countries: Madagascar, Nigeria, Kenya, Sierra Leone, Mozambique, Senegal, Uganda

Name: MILLETS in upland regions of Odisha for crop diversification, climate resilience and enhanced Food and Nutritional Security
Dscription: MILLETS in upland regions of Odisha for crop diversification, climate resilience and enhanced Food and Nutritional Security
Project Summary: The project “MILLETS in upland regions of Odisha for crop diversification, climate resilience and enhanced Food and Nutritional Security” is analysing the temporal rice cropping pattern in various ecosystems of Odisha state to identify the drought stress-prone areas in the Uplands which have high frequency of water shortage over years. Promising available varieties, hybrids and improved breeding lines of Millets are being evaluated in the targeted identified ecology to identify promising cultivars of Millets for the state, along with the identification of the cropping systems in which these millet crops can be established in the Uplands. The breeding pipeline for these Millet crops are being developed and strengthened in the Odisha state for long-term sustainability of these crops, this will be also coupled with development of standard management practices to have high productivity of Millets in the state, followed by strengthening of the seed systems of Millets in the Odisha state.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: R0230-Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptation
Dscription: Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptation from CGIAR gene banks
Project Summary: The project builds on BMGF's investment in allele mining for climate adaptation by enhancing the discovery and deployment of climate-adaptive alleles from global germplasm collections. It will expand to include common bean and extend maize deployment, focusing on breeding programs in Guatemala, Honduras, and El Salvador. The initiative aims to test integrated approaches and develop a scalable model for rapid and cost-effective deployment of climate-adaptive alleles from CGIAR genebanks.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo
Countries: Colombia, Guatemala, El Salvador, Mexico, Honduras

Name: R0223-Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptation
Dscription: Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptation from CGIAR gene banks
Project Summary: The project will support faster and more cost-effective discovery and deployment of climate-adaptive alleles from the world’s germplasm collections. It will test integrated approaches for five major crops (i.e., cassava, maize, sorghum, cowpea, and rice), providing a scalable model for the rapid and cost-effective discovery and deployment of climate-adaptive alleles.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo
Countries: Philippines, Kenya, United States of America, Mexico

Name: N-306005-NUTRITION STATUS-BIHAR&OD
Dscription: NUTRITION STATUS-BIHAR&OD
Project Summary: This program will lay the foundation to scale and mainstream biofortification globally in Bihar, Odisha, and India as a whole, serving as a springboard for proliferation of biofortified crops throughout these states and those bordering, impacting the entire value chain. For this purpose, HarvestPlus will work to compile the evidence base on the nutritional impact, cost effectiveness and scalability of biofortification throughout this process for future application. It is anticipated that this program will result in improved micronutrient intake for over a million people within five years and millions more in the years to follow and will demonstrate the capacity of agriculture to make a significant contribution in the fight against micronutrient malnutrition.

Name: R-A-2023-83-OneRicePH
Dscription: OneRicePH
Project Summary: The project will: 1) validate market-driven product concepts of provinces in 16 regions in the Philippines; 2.) Increase genetic gains in national breeding programs by 1%; 3. Deliver best genetics to national breeding programs by leveraging genes identified for major pest and disease resistances, grain quality and stress tolerances to improve Philippine rice varieties;  4. Develop consumer-driven healthier rice with low GI and high antioxidant activity for health improvement and disease prevention; and  5. Develop a product profile database for effective variety positioning and replacement, to increase varietal turnover in farmers' fields (reduction in average age of varieties currently grown), and catalyze local seed systems.

Name: D-200355-Participation Agreement for Supply of Materials
Dscription: Participation Agreement for Supply of Materials - CAIGE Project
Project Summary: The project is in partnership with the University of Sydney and works to permit Australian grain breeders access to provider sites, materials and data. Furthermore, the project works on coordinating the process of the supply of materials and data selected by Australian grain breeders including providing information on selected traits confirmed using global phenotyping platforms. Finally, the project ensures that the University of Sydney will be notified of any restrictions on the use of materials and data acknowledging the expectation that all materials selected by the University of Sydney will be made available.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: C0042 USAID-CSISA-MEA
Dscription: USAID-CSISA-MEA
