Project Summary: The Cereal Systems Initiative for South Asia – Mechanization and Irrigation (CSISA-MI) is a CIMMYT-led project in partnership with iDE under USAID’s Feed the Future (FtF) initiative, aimed at enhancing agricultural productivity in southern Bangladesh. The project focuses on promoting efficient irrigation systems, such as Axial Flow Pumps (AFPs), to enable dry-season cropping, alongside mechanization technologies like Power Tiller Operated Seeders (PTOS) and reapers to reduce labor costs and improve efficiency. CSISA-MI also fosters private sector engagement by supporting local businesses to develop a sustainable supply chain for mechanization services. By improving farm-level efficiency, expanding access to modern agricultural equipment, and encouraging climate-resilient practices, the project seeks to increase farmer incomes, enhance food security, and promote sustainable agricultural growth in Bangladesh.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: D-200369-Fertilize Right - Pakistan
Dscription: Fertilize Right - Pakistan
Project Summary:
The Fertilize Right Pakistan program, which was announced in March 2023, is a collaboration between USDA, the Pakistan Agricultural Research Council (PARC), the International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas (ICARDA), and other partners. The project prioritizes expanded use of slow-release fertilizers, coated nitrogen products, and on-farm waste-product reuse to improve nutrient efficiency -- all with a gender lens to ensure women farmers also benefit from these solutions.
The team has identified priority focus areas, including building local stakeholder groups well-represented by women, researching climate-resilient cropping systems suited to the needs of women farmers, and addressing gender barriers around adopting new agricultural technologies to ensure equitable access and participation. The Fertilize Right Pakistan team put together a theory of change analysis indicating that robust program uptake could stabilize or reduce projected agricultural emissions increases under a business-as-usual trajectory. This would set Pakistan on a pathway to meet its stated goal of a 30 percent emissions reduction from 2018 levels, while promoting gender equality in the agricultural sector.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: T-PJ-003693: Projet d’Appui au Programme Graine-Phase 1 (PAPG1) in Gabon
Dscription: PJ-003693: Projet d’Appui au Programme Graine-Phase 1 (PAPG1) in Gabon

Name: BIUSA (Bezos Earth Fund)-Using genetic diversity to capture carbon through deep root systems in tropical soils  (Research and Capacity development)
Dscription: Objecive 3. Soils and management
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: D-100865-PRIMA: Water Mellon: Harnessing water saving approaches, drought and salinity resilient species to address extreme drought in arid and semi-arid environments of the Mediterranean region to create sustainable and resilient agricultural systems on-farm
Dscription: PRIMA: Water Mellon: Harnessing water saving approaches, drought and salinity resilient species to address extreme drought in arid and semi-arid environments of the Mediterranean region to create sustainable and resilient agricultural systems on-farm
Project Summary:
WaterMellon is a multi-actor consortium of organizations aiming to improve Mediterranean dry farming's ability to withstand and recover from challenges by combining traditional water-harvesting methods with modern agricultural techniques. The initiative aims to achieve sustainable food and feed production and improve the livelihoods of smallholder farmers by providing training to local farmers, harnessing traditional water structures, and applying novel cropping practices tailored for arid environments. 
The project aims to engage the farming community and the relevant stakeholders (policy makers, land owners, end-users) in the demo fields, to train and enable them to develop regional living labs. The project incorporates modern water harvesting techniques along with inspired by traditional practices (rainwater harvesting, atmospheric moisture capture) and rehabilitate ancestral structures. In addition, the project develops and evaluates novel cropping systems (NCSs) tailored for arid and semi-arid and/or saline soils in the Med region including underutilized crops. Finally, the project develops demonstration sites in 6 Med countries (Italy, Greece, Morocco, Algeria, Spain and Turkey) and works on developing a robust and tailored dissemination and communication plan (DCP) combined with a results’ 
exploitation plan.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: D-100894- UAE -Red Palm Weevil
Dscription: to be confirmed
Project Summary:
The ultimate design of the program will be informed by the comprehensive analysis of existing efforts, stakeholder mapping and consultation, and strategic planning to design a three-year program and detailed results framework. Early-stage thinking on potential outcomes include:
•Global Red Palm Weevil Consortium established and operationalized.
•All date palm trees GIS tagged, data and maps generated.
•The productivity of date palm increased by 25% per year using sensor based Integrated RPW implemented in all date palm farms.
•Effective natural enemies released as components of integrated RPW management.
•All date palm farmers use Biochar and drip irrigation in date palm production.
•Capacity of stakeholder’s strengthened through academic and non-academic training provided.
•Digital extension in date palm production used by all date palm farmers.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2022-118-Credits Offsetting Rice Emissions
Dscription: Credits Offsetting
Project Summary: The project´s core objective is to facilitate enabling conditions for changing smallholder farmers and value chain actors´ access to the carbon market. This will be achieved by improving the evidence-base for combined emission reduction practices, developing a new methodology for generating carbon credits from shifting current crop management, analyzing emissions through post-harvest losses, guiding policy and developing support tools for partner organizations’ extension teams and farmers based on needs.

Name: V0145-Sustainable AgriFood Systems Approach for Sudan - SA
Dscription: Sustainable AgriFood Systems Approach for Sudan - SASAS
Project Summary: The SASAS Program, led by CIMMYT and funded by USAID under the Feed the Future Initiatives, was initially designed in 2022. Following the outbreak of war in Sudan on April 15, 2023, SASAS pivoted to focus on enhancing food security by building resilient agrifood systems. Key interventions include promoting smallholder agricultural production with improved technologies, boosting value chains and business development, managing natural resources for social cohesion, improving horticultural practices, and establishing livestock services. These efforts are aimed at strengthening the agricultural value chain and supporting Sudanese communities in a conflict-affected environment.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: 1475-GIZ0  Potato production through zero-tillage with straw mulch: an innovative technology for sustainable intensification and diversification of rice-based systems to improve livelihoods of small-scale farmers in Asia
Dscription: Potato production through zero-tillage with straw mulch: an innovative technology for sustainable intensification and diversification of rice-based systems to improve livelihoods of small-scale farmers in Asia
Project Summary:
The overall objective of the project is defined by the following benchmarks:
For 2000 smallholder farmers, which have applied PZTM, the gross margin has increased by 15%.
In 50% of 1000 soil samples 2 out of 6 selected indicators of soil quality have improved.
1000 women confirm that their position as agricultural producers in the rice-potato cultivation system has improved.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: A-HP2023-003-World Bank-Food Systems Resilience
Dscription: World Bank-Food Systems Resilience
Project Summary: The World Bank-Food Systems Resilience project, implemented by AfricaRice, is a comprehensive initiative aimed at bolstering the resilience of agricultural and food systems across Africa. Recognizing the growing impact of climate change and environmental pressures on food security, the project seeks to address these challenges by promoting adaptive and sustainable practices in agriculture. Central to the project is the focus on climate-smart agriculture, which involves the adoption of innovative technologies and practices designed to mitigate the effects of climate change while enhancing productivity. This includes the development and dissemination of resilient rice varieties that can withstand extreme weather conditions, such as droughts and floods. The project also supports the implementation of efficient irrigation systems and advanced soil management techniques to optimize resource use and improve crop yields.
Project results: (1) Regional food security enhancement; (2) Climate-resilient qgricultural practices: the program has promoted the adoption of climate-smart agriculture, leading to increased crop yields and resilience against climate change; (3) Infrastructure Development.

Name: D-200364-Wheat Disease Early Warning Advisory System DEWAS
Dscription: Wheat Disease Early Warning Advisory System DEWAS - Diagnostics
Project Summary: The Wheat Disease Early Warning Advisory System (DEWAS) project aims to achieve a scalable and sustainable monitoring and early warning advisory system for key wheat diseases in East Africa and South Asia; for better preparedness, contingency planning, and control. This investment proposes to investigate the right mix of science technologies and partners, align stakeholders, and implement a low-cost, open, and accessible system that works for current user needs as well as easily scales to new users. In addition, the project targets strengthening national capacities relating to surveillance, diagnostics, and models. The project adopts the methodology of having coordinated standardized surveillance including digitally enabled crowd sourcing approaches. This will result in improved knowledge of host landscape vulnerability (disease resistance status in farmers’ fields in space and time), integrated with pathogen and environment information (disease triangle concept) making those scenarios and strategies available to policy makers.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: V0156-Wheat Disease Early Warning Advisory System (Wheat D
Dscription: Wheat Disease Early Warning Advisory System (Wheat DEWAS)
Project Summary: The purpose of this project is to deploy a scalable and sustainable system for mitigation of biotic risks to wheat with rust and blast diseases the biggest concern.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo
Countries: Mexico, Bhutan, Zambia, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Ethiopia, Nepal, Kenya

Name: W-D-0424-WRAP C1
Dscription: Water Resource Accountability in Pakistan (WRAP) Component 1
Project Summary: The long-term goal of Component 1 is to strengthen capacity to manage water resources at Federal, Provincial and District levels. Potential institutions that will be supported under the project include the Provincial Planning and Development Department, water resources commissions, water regulatory authorities, irrigation, and on-farm water management departments. This Component will also improve the enabling environment for water sector reforms through better accountability, monitoring and narrative setting. Women, youth, and marginalized people will be specifically targeted with their engagement.
Project results: IWMI is implementing Component 1 of the Water Resource Accountability in Pakistan (WRAP) project. The long-term goal of this Component is to strengthen capacity to manage water resources at federal, provincial and district levels. Potential institutions that will be supported under the project include the Provincial Planning and Development Department, water resources commissions, water regulatory authorities, irrigation, and on-farm water management departments. This Component will also improve the enabling environment for water sector reforms through better accountability, monitoring and narrative setting. Women, youth, and marginalized people will be specifically targeted with their engagement, inputs and roles.

Name: W0500-Verify global potential of biologic. Nitrific. inhib
Dscription: Verify global potential of biologic. Nitrific. inhibition
Project Summary: The purpose of the Project is to accelerate breeding and deployment of novel wheat lines with greatly improved nitrogen-use efficiency due to root exudation of nitrification-inhibiting secondary metabolites.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: Fertilize Right Colombia
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: PJ-003897: Soil Values
Dscription: IFDC/IITA - SOIL VALUES PROJECTS (RAFS) - PJ-3897
Project Summary: The Netherlands Directorate-General for International Cooperation (DGIS) Soil Fertility Grant Program (SFGP) aims to enhance soil fertility and food productivity in the Sahel, focusing on small-scale food producers. This Soil Values initiative, part of this program, seeks to integrate soil fertility management with market development and policy support, fostering widespread adoption of sustainable practices. Key outputs include improved soil health, scalable investment mechanisms and strengthened stakeholder collaboration. Over the next decade, Soil Values aims to enhance soil fertility across 2 million hectares and support 1.5 million small-scale producers in Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger and Northern Nigeria, with regional integration efforts extending to Ghana and Côte d’Ivoire.
Project results: Enhance seed system functionality to meet smallholder farmers' needs, increasing productivity, market access, and the availability of nutrient-dense foods.

Name: PJ-003938: Food Systems Resilience Program interventions in Feed Salone
Dscription: MAFFS/IITA - FOOD SYSTEMS RESILIENCE PROGRAM (FSRP) INTERVENTIONS IN FEED SALONE (SIERRA LEONE) (RAFS) PJ-3938
Project results: Introduce mechanized irrigation, develop a sustainable seed and input system, and enhance value chains for inclusive growth. Create business and partnership opportunities, develop business plans for women and youth, and provide regular reports on processes, activities, and business models.

Name: R-A-2024-191 AGRI
Dscription: Accelerating development and scaling of agricultural innovations for reducing  GHG emissions in ASEAN countries
Project Summary: This project will install new gas chromatography (known as GC) in the soil laboratory, IRRI Headquarters in Los Baños, Philippines. The GC will be used for training programs and the provision of GHG measurement services. Furthermore, training on data analytics, including the application of modeling work that will support scaling of the Tier 3 approach in GHG estimates, will be organized to enhance the capacity of IRRI staff and partners in analyzing GHG emission data and optimizing rice-based farming systems. The training program will be open to participants from countries beyond the ASEAN region, while the GC service will primarily focus on supporting activities within the Philippines.
Project results: Accelerate the development and scaling of agricultural innovations for reducing GHG emissions in ASEAN countries

Name: V0146-AID-I : Southern Africa
Dscription: AID-I : Southern Africa
Project Summary: The Southern Africa Accelerated Innovation Delivery Initiative (AID-I) Rapid Delivery Hub, led by USAID under Feed the Future, is a two-year rapid response effort to mitigate the impact of high fuel and fertilizer prices on farmers. Targeting up to three million smallholder farmers in Malawi, Tanzania, and Zambia, AID-I improves soil health, fertilizer management, seed systems, financial access, and extension services. It establishes agile rural innovation hubs to enhance technology adaptation, sustainable input and output development, and value chain diversification. The project focuses on scaling resilient agronomic practices and strengthening rural-urban linkages for better national cross-network management.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo
Countries: Malawi, Lesotho, Namibia, Mozambique, Botswana, Eswatini, South Africa, Zambia

Name: Bihar Centre Of Excellence For Millets Value Chain (BCoEMVC)
Dscription: Bihar Centre Of Excellence For Millets Value Chain (BCoEMVC)
Project Summary:
ICRISAT, in collaboration with the Government of Bihar, will establish a “Centre of Excellence for Millets and Value Chain (CoE-MVC)”, in collaboration with the Government of Bihar will establish a “Centre of Excellence for Millets and Value Chain (CoE-MVC)”, to promote research, breeding, production, mechanization, and value chain as a long-term vision for sustainable promotion of millets to diversify its crop and food systems. Overall, this concept aims to enhance Bihar’s crop and food systems by addressing the challenges of Bihar state with respect to nutritional deficiencies, food insecurity, climate risk and low farm income through crop diversification and value chain development activities. 
As part of the project, ICRISAT will conduct a techno-economic feasibility study and develop Ready-to-Cook (RTC) and Ready-to-Eat (RTE) millet products, which will be integrated into the state's mid-day meal program.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: A-AG10181-BMGF MultiHarvestRice
Dscription: BMGF MultiHarvestRice
Project Summary: The MultiHarvestRice project, led by AfricaRice, is a transformative initiative aimed at enhancing rice production across Africa through the introduction of high-yielding and climate-resilient rice varieties. The project addresses the critical need for increased rice productivity and sustainability in the face of growing environmental challenges and food security concerns. A central component of the project is the development and promotion of advanced rice varieties that are not only high-yielding but also resilient to climate-related stresses such as droughts, floods, and heat. These new varieties are designed to thrive in diverse environmental conditions, thereby reducing the risks associated with climate variability and improving overall crop stability. By providing farmers with access to these improved varieties, the project helps to boost rice yields and ensure more reliable production.
Project results: A-AG10181-BMGF MultiHarvestRice

Name: T-PJ-003871-Making effective bioinputs work for smallholder farmers in SSA (Bio-SSA)
Dscription: BMGF/IITA - MAKING EFFECTIVE BIOINPUTS WORK F
Project Summary: This project aims to enhance the efficiency, productivity and profitability of smallholder cropping systems in Nigeria and Tanzania through effective bioinputs, focusing on nitrogen (N) and phosphorus (P) dynamics. Over its 48-month duration, the first two years will gather evidence on microbial strains, while the latter years will develop high-quality products and user instructions. Led by the International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA), in partnership with national and international organizations, this project will assess microbial strains for grain legumes and root tubers, with a focus on developing bioinput products that improve N and P acquisition. This project will also explore combining microbials to enhance biofertilization rates, leveraging existing knowledge from successful products like Nodumax.
Project results: Enhance bioinput adoption to improve efficiency, productivity, and profitability of African smallholder cropping systems, focusing on N and P dynamics, under a changing climate in Nigeria, Tanzania, and beyond.

Name: D-100894-BMGF: Preparatory phase for the Red Palm Weevil project
Dscription: BMGF: Preparatory phase for the Red Palm Weevil project
Project Summary:
The project aims at improving the productivity and quality of date palm for sustainable food security, income generation and environmental health. The project will have four main components supported by cross-cutting activities:
Component 1: Formation of global Consortium: Bring partners skills, knowledge, and technologies from consortium members and partners to bring Sustainable Red Palm Weevil (RPW) management.
Component 2: Creating an Inventory of deployable, diverse and scalable RBW innovations for early detection, monitoring, and management options of RPM with their own strengths and weaknesses. 
Component 3: Validation of innovations: These will be done on a large scale during the first year of the project implementation under different farm sizes in the UAE. Entomopathogenic fungi, bacteria and nematodes with better delivery system will deploy in managing RPW.  
Component 4: Integration & deployment of conventional, existing and validated emerging innovations to be deployed throughout UAE phase by phase during the four years project cycles
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: T-PJ-003255-Building Economically Sustainable Seed Systems f
Dscription: Building Economically Sustainable Seed Systems for Cassava (BASICS-II)
Project results: The BASICS-II Project successfully strengthened cassava seed systems in Nigeria and Tanzania, establishing sustainable seed enterprises, supporting decentralized cassava seed entrepreneurs (CSEs), and engaging processors in seed production. Policies and regulatory frameworks were enhanced, and new varieties gained rapid adoption. Collaboration across partners ensured quality control and market linkages. The model was extended to Sierra Leone and Liberia, with SeedTracker playing a key role in monitoring and sustainability efforts.

Name: D-200340-CARINA - CARinata and CamelINA to boost the sustain
Dscription: CARINA - CARinata and CamelINA to boost the sustainable diversification in EU farming systems
Project Summary:
CARINA is built on a multi-actor consortium and participative decision-making process through mutual learning, transparent communication, and inclusive multi-perspectives and transdisciplinary engagement. Nine Lighthouses, 5 Living Labs, and 9 Policy Innovation Labs will be established across Europe playing a leading role in the co-creation of CARINA innovation actions. CARINA focuses on new sustainable and diversified farming systems including 2 new oilseed crops, carinata and camelina, able to provide multiple low iLUC feedstocks for the bio-based economy. Through CARINA partnerships, there is an estimate of around 3M farmers being potentially reached by the project. 
CARINA capitalizes on a highly experienced team of 20 partners, +6 affiliated entities, from 13 EU and Associated Countries. Carinata and camelina provide high quality oils that will be transformed into innovative bio-based products (bioherbicides, bioplastics). The co-product from oil extraction is a protein-rich cake, which will be valorized as animal feed, and in a multitude of high added-value products, exploiting the mucilage and glucosinolates contained within in a circular and bioeconomy economy.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: 1414-EC00 DESIRA
Dscription: DeSIRA- Climate-smart innovations to improve productivity, profitability, and sustainability of agriculture and food systems in Malawi through multidisciplinary research
Project Summary: The goal of the project is to improve productivity, profitability and sustainability of agriculture and food systems in Malawi. Activities will aim at achieving three specific objectives. 1) develop climate-smart integrated technological innovations that can contribute to enhanced resilience of agricultural and food systems; 2) gaining insights in pathways to adoption of technological innovation; 3) information that is of interest to policy makers and scaling partner will be generated and shared with local, national, and international policy makers and scaling partners.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: Developing and introducing high-yielding climate-resilient pigeon pea cultivars for intensification and diversification of cropping systems and to satisfy the nutritional requirement of small holder farmers of Odisha
Dscription: Developing and introducing high-yielding climate-resilient pigeon pea cultivars for intensification and diversification of cropping systems and to satisfy the nutritional requirement of small holder farmers of Odisha
Project Summary:
To promote high-yielding climate-resilient pigeonpea cultivars for intensification of cropping systems and to meet the nutritional and other livelihood needs of small-holder farmers in Odisha
1. Strengthen the pigeonpea breeding program of the Centre for Pulses Research (CPR), Berhampur and RRTTS, Bhawanipatna, from OUAT by developing a strong pigeonpea breeding pipeline.
2. Modernize pigeonpea breeding operations of Centre for Pulses Research (CPR), Berhampur, and RRTTS, Bhawanipatna from OUAT
3. Identify, evaluate and release high-yielding, fusarium wilt and sterility mosaic-resistant varieties and hybrids of pigeonpea in early and mid-early duration and well adapted to cropping systems of the target districts
4. Evaluate the potential of newly developed photo-insensitive super-early pigeonpea varieties with market-preferred traits in rice fallows
5. Promote the adoption of farmer and market-preferred varieties and hybrids of pigeonpea with Improved Pigeonpea Production Technologies (IPPT) through citizen science approach “triadic comparisons of technologies(tricot).”
6. To build the capacity of farmers and Farmer Producers Organizations (FPOs) in seed production of farmer-preferred improved varieties for improving the availability of genetically pure seed.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: T-PJ-003188-Development and promotion of aflatoxin biologica
Dscription: Development and promotion of aflatoxin biological control in the groundnut, sorghum, sesame, and cottonseed value chains to improve publ

Name: D-200371-Development and Scaling of Introduced Plant Species
Dscription: Development and Scaling of Introduced Plant Species for Food and Feed Crops with a Functional Seed Delivery System in Abu Dhabi
Project Summary:
The main objective of this project is to increase the availability and to ensure the sustainability of high value feed and food crop production with drought, heat, and salinity tolerant species for different agroecological zones in Abu Dhabi. 
The studies are to be carried through participatory adaptive research in a systemic mode of operation covering the different aspects of soil, water, climate, plant, seed, livestock and socio-economic factors. The studies aim to investigate in conventional and alternative fodder and food crop productivity under the prevalent desert farming ecosystems of Abu Dhabi, covering the basic crop management practices under the prevalent biotic and abiotic constraints of water, soil fertility, pests and socio-economic conditions.
By the end of the project, the project research team will release a report consisting of a list of adapted germplasms to the Abu Dhabi environmental conditions with the associated agriculture inputs, agronomic package, the appropriate post-harvest operations, biomass and seed yields, feed quality attributes, socioeconomic benefits and value chain analysis.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2022-52-Direct Seeding Indo Gangetic
Dscription: Direct Seeding Indo Gangetic
Project Summary: The project will introduce a planned deployment of haplotypes essential to high performance under reduced-irrigation Dry Direct Seeding. This is achieved in parallel to quantitative breeding efforts through IRRI´s centralized, demand-driven trait development pipeline, which is designed to deliver robust, large-effect alleles needed for specific market segments/TPEs in a form that breeders can easily incorporate without the penalties usually associated with introducing new genetics to elite material.

Name: R-A-2023-20-DSR-India
Dscription: DSR-India
Project Summary: The project focuses on increasing the productivity, profitability, and environmental sustainability of rice-based systems in India. It aims to do this by promoting the adoption of mechanized dry direct-seeded rice (DSR) in selected rice-producing states. The project seeks to address challenges such as labor and water shortages, rising cultivation costs, and climate change impacts. The project will be implemented over three years in 18 districts across eight Indian states.

Name: N-632001-EFF CASH TXFR ON COFFE PR
Dscription: EFF CASH TXFR ON COFFE PR
Project Summary: The objective of this project is to provide a short overview of proposed research designs for an evaluation analyzing the effect of cash transfers delivered by GiveDirectly to coffee farmers in Sidama region, Ethiopia. Coffee plays a central role in the Ethiopian economy; it is the most important export crop, and is grown by more than four million smallholders. Evidence suggests that coffee production enhances food security for households that grow it. However, coffee yields in Ethiopia remain relatively low (around 30% below the worldwide mean), and these relatively low yields may reflect in part poor farm management and agronomic practices. The key research questions for this project include: 1) What are the effects of cash transfers on a range of economic outcomes for coffee producing households, including: investment in coffee production and coffee yield and profits; investment in other economic activities; assets, consumption, and poverty? 2) How do these effects vary with respect to the timing of the cash transfer (in the lean season vis-à-vis in the key season for coffee investments and/or vis-à-vis a chosen disbursement schedule)? 3) Are the effects of transfers of various timings also heterogeneous for households that have different time preferences at baseline, such that households that are more impatient show more positive effects of a transfer timed to key investment opportunities?

Name: Enhancing groundnut productivity through increased quality seed access in Odisha
Dscription: Enhancing groundnut productivity through increased quality seed access in Odisha
Project Summary:
The overall objective of the project is to increase access to quality groundnut seed for cultivation in the state.
Specific goals:
1. Train the farmers in groundnut seed production.
2. Increase the production and supply of quality groundnut seed.
3. Replace old and obsolete varieties with new improved groundnut varieties.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: T-PJ-003835-EU/IITA - FOOD SAFETY FOR AFRICA - ADDRESSING FO
Dscription: EU/IITA - FOOD SAFETY FOR AFRICA - ADDRESSING FOOD SAFETY CHALLENGES IN THE AFRICAN INFORMAL SECTOR THROUGH INNOVATIVE STRATEGIES & USE CASES (RAFS)-PJ-3835...T.FALADE
Project results: Generate GAP analysis and landscape reports on the informal sector with evidence-based recommendations. Develop food safety knowledge platforms, directories, and evaluation reports. Establish communication, advocacy, and innovation plans, alongside ethical committee setup, work plans, and data management guidelines.
Countries: Egypt, South Africa, Cameroon, Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana

Name: D-200329-FARMS4CLIMATE- Smart Governance and Operational Mod
Dscription: FARMS4CLIMATE- Smart Governance and Operational Models for Agroecological Carbon Farming
Project Summary:
The main objective of FARMS4CLIMATE consortium is making carbon farming operational for smallholders. This will be made possible by the ad hoc development of a number of digital enablers, including the creation of a ready-to-market C credit platform, smart tools to monitor, report and validate the various C pools at the farm level, a system to account and exchange C credits along the value chain and, short distribution chains where the differential value propositions are based on virtuous C management.
The FARMS4CLIMATE consortium executes living labs with such a strategic approach in five different Mediterranean locations (two in Italy, one in Egypt, one in Tunisia and, one in Spain). Each program will focus on a specific regenerative practice, aiming at creating five Community Based Organizations (CBOs) that can drive economic growth by flagshipping virtuous carbon management embedded into agroecological principles and thanks to frugal innovations.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2023-56
Dscription: Fertilize Right Vietnam
Project Summary: This project will introduce the integrated interventions of technologies and practices, capacity development, and enabling policies in favor of innovations of precision crop establishment to reduce seed and fertilizer use, site-specific nutrient management, and low-carbon and organic alternatives to chemical fertilizers. These interventions would substantially contribute to the following goal and objectives of the USDA’s Fertilize Right project i) increased fertilizer-use efficiency, (ii) enhanced fertilizer effectiveness, and (iii) developed alternatives to traditional manufactured chemical fertilizers to improve soil health and soil fertility.

Name: D-100777-GCC Date Palm
Dscription: GCC Date Palm Project
Project Summary: The proposed program envisions to increase the adoption of appropriate desert farming innovation packages and evidence-based investment in desert farming by public and private sectors. This will be done through establishment of Innovation Platforms (IPs) which will be physically located in strategically selected locations considering the mobility zones of stakeholders across the relevant agribusiness landscape. Main and satellite IPs will be developed, operated and managed. The main IPs are full-fledged integrated research and development sites managed by networks of collaborators across ecosystems within one of the three prevalent production systems in the region, namely seasonal crops, fruit trees and livestock and feed.  The program will also establish and strengthen partnerships and capacities and devise policy pathways for sustainable transformation to address common challenges in desert farming. Additionally, the project will support inclusive entrepreneurship through an incubator component for capacity development of youth and women.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas
Countries: Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, The United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman

Name: R-A-2022-71-ICAR 2023 - 2027
Project Summary: The project has 5 thematic work areas, namely: 1) Establish a framework for developing market oriented rice varieties with higher genetic gains using IRRI’s OneRice Breeding Strategy; 2) Integrate direct seeded rice system for India by combining the strengths of DSR-breeding with best agronomic practices, with special emphasis on the Eastern India; 3) Rice-based Health and Nutrition through improved starch quality to lower the glycemic index (GI) and increased nutrient density in the milled rice through breeding interventions; 4) Climate Resilient Rice through the development of agronomic practices that help to mitigate the effects of rice cultivation on the climate, specifically in terms of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.; 5) Integrated Capacity Building.

Name: T-PJ-003763-IITA Accelerated Innovation Delivery Initiative
Dscription: IITA Accelerated Innovation Delivery Initiative – AID-I: Great Lakes Highlands
Project Summary: The Accelerated Innovation Delivery Initiative in the Great Lakes Region (AID-I GLR) is a two-year initiative funded by USAID to enhance food and nutrition security in Burundi, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda. It aims to reach 1.2 million farmers by rapidly scaling up proven agricultural technologies and innovations developed primarily by CGIAR and partners. This initiative focuses on creating and structuring partnerships with the private sector, government agencies and NGOs to deliver these technologies, adapted to local needs, along with advisory services to a large network of farmers. AID-I GLR is part of the U.S. response to the global food security crisis, targeting both immediate and long-term impacts on food security in the region.
Project results: Two new partnerships were established in Burundi and DRC, and nine market actors enrolled. The project reached 774,965 participants (50% women, 22% youth), exceeding targets. Over 600,000 smallholder farmers received quality seeds, and 92,000 accessed fortified maize flour. Scaling events engaged 350,000 farmers, 770 demonstration plots were established, and 99,000 stakeholders trained. Additionally, 147.2 metric tons of Early Generation Seeds were produced, and market actors sold 8,200 Mt of inputs worth $3.6 million.

Name: UNITED KINGDOM - FCDO -iSPARK: Innovation in sustainability, policy, adaptation and resilience in Kenya (IA Lead of D511)
Dscription: In Kenya, environmental degradation and climate change threaten the sustainability and resilience of smallholder farming systems. Farmers need timely and accurate climate information combined with agro-advisories to make informed decisions. Meanwhile, policy makers need evidence to prioritize policy choices and investment decisions that contribute to environmental sustainability, climate resilience, and other development goals by mid-century. iSPARK is designed to tailor evidence towards supporting appropriate and contextualised advisory services, policies, and investment in equity-sensitive sustainable and climate-smart innovation in western Kenya, building on CGIAR Initiatives on Climate Resilience and Excellence in Agronomy. The core of iSPARK is developing and using a metric-based approach called Innovation in Sustainability, Resilience, and Adaptation (ISRA) to provide specific evidence for innovations and pathways at different levels, from individual farms to regional policies. iSPARK aims to integrate ISRA metrics into agro-advisories reaching 100,000 farmers; create/support at least one plan/policy; and create/support at least one investment case or funding proposal by 2026.
Project Summary: In Kenya, environmental degradation and climate change threaten the sustainability and resilience of smallholder farming systems. Farmers need timely and accurate climate information combined with agro-advisories to make informed decisions. Meanwhile, policy makers need evidence to prioritize policy choices and investment decisions that contribute to environmental sustainability, climate resilience, and other development goals by mid-century. iSPARK is designed to tailor evidence towards supporting appropriate and contextualised advisory services, policies, and investment in equity-sensitive sustainable and climate-smart innovation in western Kenya, building on CGIAR Initiatives on Climate Resilience and Excellence in Agronomy. The core of iSPARK is developing and using a metric-based approach called Innovation in Sustainability, Resilience, and Adaptation (ISRA) to provide specific evidence for innovations and pathways at different levels, from individual farms to regional policies. iSPARK aims to integrate ISRA metrics into agro-advisories reaching 100,000 farmers; create/support at least one plan/policy; and create/support at least one investment case or funding proposal by 2026.
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: Increasing Pulses Productivity and Incomes through Science-led Interventions in rice fallows of Odisha
Dscription: Increasing Pulses Productivity and Incomes through Science-led Interventions in rice fallows of Odisha
Project Summary:
The overall goal of the mission is to establish exemplar sites of learning demonstrating increased average productivity of agricultural systems in the selected districts of Odisha. The specific objectives are as follows:
1. On-farm participatory evaluation of an early maturing variety of chickpea, pigeonpea and green gram in target districts.
2. Enhancing rice fallows' crop productivity and income by adopting the best bet agronomic practices such as zero tillage and soil test-based micronutrient application.
3. To build the capacity of the farmers and stakeholders in the sustainable management of rice fallows in identified districts of Odisha.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: D-200366-Innovative Desert Farming for Resilient Livelihoods
Dscription: Innovative Desert Farming for Resilient Livelihoods - IDFRL
Project Summary: The Innovative Desert Farming for Resilient Livelihoods project aims to realize enhanced resilience and sustainable productivity of desert agricultural systems for increased income and food security. The project works on Increasing adoption of sustainable desert farming technologies and innovative practices for enhanced productivity and improved livelihoods of rural communities. The project has identified a suite of technologies to equitably meet the needs of farmers across the socio-economic ladder serving a total of 3,450 medium to large-scale farmers across the 5 targeted governorates. In addition, the project enhances awareness and knowledge of relevant stakeholders to facilitate wide-scale adoption of recommended technologies adapted to desert agriculture. The project will partner with three major research institutions in Egypt: ARC, NWRC, and DRC. The institutions have well-established research stations that can be used by the project in the selected governorates. The institutions will be instrumental in providing linkages with the target communities, availing staff and equipment for project use, and in ensuring the sustainable continuity of the project results beyond its duration.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2024-19-ISARC Phase III
Dscription: ISARC Phase II
Project Summary: The ISARC program will increase farmers’ incomes, improve food and nutrition security, health, and well-being of smallholder farmers through increased system productivity, reduced yield gaps, enhanced climate resilience, mechanized and digital farming, improved market linkages, modernized value chains enabling entrepreneurship for women and youth, and capacity development.

Name: R-A-2024-25-Madhya Pradesh_Rice Inbreds and Hybrids
Dscription: Madhya Pradesh_Rice Inbreds and Hybrids
Project Summary: The project aims to (1) develop an annual plan and strategy for sustainable and low carbon rice transformation in target countries; (2) develop training materials on scale-appropriate sustainable and low carbon rice (including hands-on manual, e-learning, and videos); 3) enhance knowledge and adoption of sustainable and low carbon rice through training, field demonstrations, and technology transfers.

Name: R-A-2023-67-MHRA
Dscription: MHRA
Project Summary: The project aims to identify and evaluate rice varieties and management practices to promote multiple-harvest rice production. This system offers a low-cost, labor-saving intensification approach for rice-based cropping systems in Sub-Saharan Africa. It focuses on adapting rice varieties and management strategies to increase profitability, reduce production costs, and support climate change adaptation in African rice systems. By utilizing ratooning, the project seeks to maximize rice yield and reduce labor inputs, benefiting small-scale producers, especially women farmers.
Countries: Mozambique, Uganda, Kenya, Nigeria, Sierra Leone, Madagascar, Senegal

Name: W-D-0392-NET: WaPOR Phase 2
Dscription: Monitoring land and water productivity by Remote Sensing (WaPOR Phase 2)
Project Summary:
The outcome of the project is defined as “Increased capacity of FAO Members (Member Nations and Member Organizations) in monitoring terrestrial biomass production and evapotranspiration to assess and improve land and water productivity in rainfed and irrigated agriculture, and contribute to sustainable crop production intensification adapted to a changing climate.”

The three major Outputs of the project are:
1. Consolidated open-access information and related operational services on the terrestrial soil water balance and agricultural land and water productivity, including refinement and expansion towards global coverage of the spatial scales, and creation of longer time series (Spatial database);
2. Strengthened capacities of FAO Members to assess land and water productivity aimed at informed decision making for improved agricultural practices and efficient water management by multiple stakeholder groups to minimize impacts and adapt to climate change and other environmental and societal changes (Capacity development); and
3. Compendium of implementable solutions and tools, co-developed with various stakeholders at different operational scales, from farm to the national level, to effectively increase land and water productivity in agriculture sustainably at field level as well as to support water management, water governance and agricultural policies (Compendium of implementable solutions and tools).
Project results:
The outcome of the project is defined as “Increased capacity of FAO Members (Member Nations and Member Organizations) in monitoring terrestrial biomass production and evapotranspiration to assess and improve land and water productivity in rainfed and irrigated agriculture, and contribute to sustainable crop production intensification adapted to a changing climate.”

The three major Outputs of the project are:
1. Consolidated open-access information and related operational services on the terrestrial soil water balance and agricultural land and water productivity, including refinement and expansion towards global coverage of the spatial scales, and creation of longer time series (Spatial database);
2. Strengthened capacities of FAO Members to assess land and water productivity aimed at informed decision making for improved agricultural practices and efficient water management by multiple stakeholder groups to minimize impacts and adapt to climate change and other environmental and societal changes (Capacity development); and
3. Compendium of implementable solutions and tools, co-developed with various stakeholders at different operational scales, from farm to the national level, to effectively increase land and water productivity in agriculture sustainably at field level as well as to support water management, water governance and agricultural policies (Compendium of implementable solutions and tools).
Countries: Kenya, Mozambique, Algeria, Iraq, Ethiopia, Pakistan, Mali, Jordan, Tunisia

Name: R-A-2024-130 CRFM
Dscription: Odisha Rice Fallow Management
Project Summary:
IRRI's key role is to manage the roll-out or dissemination and demonstration of fallow crops like Green gram, Black Gram, Mustard and Sesame in fallow areas (i.e.,areas lying unused in between two rice cropping seasons) in 44,500 hectares and to support the monitoring and evaluation of this roll-out.
- The Government of Odisha prefers CG Centres to implement this annual project. This is the second time IRRI has been commissioned/selected to implement this. The MOA is similar to the one signed previously.
- Other CGIAR Centres that will implement this project are ICRISAT, ICRAF, and ICARDA. The Government of Odisha will fund them separately.

Name: T-PJ-003866-Liberia Land and Soil Resources Knowledge project (Soils4Liberia)
Dscription: PJ-003866: Liberia Land and Soil Resources Knowledge project (Soils4Liberia)
Project Summary: This project aims to develop a Land & Soil Information System for Liberia to enhance land and soil management. Its main objectives are to create a functional system with reliable data, increase stakeholder awareness and support decision-making for resource conservation, sustainable agriculture and climate resilience. Key outputs include protocols, a comprehensive database, soil property maps, a land use map, Soil Quality Indicator (SQI) maps and land and crop suitability maps. These will aid government agencies, research institutions and NGOs by providing crucial data for policy development, agricultural planning and climate change mitigation. This project seeks to improve land and soil health and support sustainable development.
Project results: Develop standard protocols and tools for soil and land use inventory and field campaign management, including a database with primary soil and land use data, soil property maps, land use maps of Liberia, Soil Quality Indicator (SQI) maps, and land and crop suitability maps.

Name: D-200394-Program 2- International host / pathogen Differential Sets:
Dscription: Program 2- International host / pathogen Differential Sets: Effective Genetic and Sustinable Management of Ascochyta Blight of Chickpea - Phase 2
Project Summary:
TRANSFORMED aims to support and promote a largescale adoption of successful agroforestry systems (AFS) in saline and degraded areas to restore soil health, increase biodiversity and productivity of the agroecosystems by overcoming the identified barriers for large-scale implementation.
The project raises awareness and facilitates knowledge transfer and capacity building of the potential of AFS for soil restoration and productivity for farmers and farmers associations, researchers, NGOs, private sector and policy makers. In addition, the project develops and strengthens governance arrangements at community level while co-developing a knowledge sharing and innovation upscaling strategy capitalizing on the existing and new knowledge created to maximize the successful adoption of AFS and a broad multistakeholder support. TRANSFORMED will build up on already AFS designs practiced by pioneer farmers or investigated at on-farm research trials that have been proven to increase the productivity of highly degraded land and improve soil health.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: D-200368-Project for Enhancement of Agriculture Productivity
Dscription: Project for Enhancement of Agriculture Productivity
Project Summary: The project aims to scale out a package of combined water-saving technologies, soil health management techniques, on-farm affordable renewable energy solutions, agronomic practices, and improved spatially explicit agronomic recommendations that are adapted to the local context, in three governorates: Minya, representing Upper Egypt; Fayoum, representing Middle Egypt; and Sharkia representing the Delta region. This will ultimately empower rural communities in Egypt to withstand current shocks by strengthening the agricultural productive capacity of scarce and fragile natural resources in Minya, Fayoum, and Sharkia Governorates. The project will equip 15 farms with low-energy drip irrigation and provide 9 scalable light-weight raised bed machines (RBM) for smallholder farming to cultivate 150 Feddans of main strategic crops. The project will also serve around 150 beneficiary farmers with about 12,000 meter-length of improved marwas in three irrigation command areas. Finally, the project will shade up around 1,500 meter-length of marwas in three irrigation command areas by solar panels.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: R-A-2022-80-REMET
Dscription: Reducing methane emissions from rice: from mechanistic understanding to scalable crop management options (REMET)
Project Summary: The project will link scientific upstream research on methanogenesis to the development of practical and scalable crop management recommendations to be used by farmers in the field. Innovative practices will be geared toward maximizing multiple benefits by aligning low methane emissions with carbon sequestration as well as with improved agronomic performance. In terms of cropping systems, the project will focus on wetland rice in rice-rice and rice-maize rotations (as general proxy for rice-upland crop rotations) typical for many rice-growing environments of Asia (especially the major delta areas). New mechanistic and empirical findings will be encapsulated in deterministic and process-based simulation modelling to allow for extrapolation and prediction under well-defined environmental conditions (current and future climates).

Name: 1523-BMGF  RTB Breeding Investment
Dscription: RTB Breeding Investment
Project Summary:
In Africa, Root, Tuber, and Banana (RTB) crops (banana, cassava, potato, sweetpotato and yam) play a major role alleviating poverty, improving food security and climate resilience, and providing better livelihoods to farmers. Though plant breeding is a proven approach to develop varieties tailored to meet customer needs, to achieve success a relentless market-driven stance, constant modernization to increase genetic gains delivered on farmer’s fields, and links with seed systems to accelerate adoption of novel varieties are of essence. RTB Breeding, a BMGF investment focused on serving African farmers, aims to:
● Progressively streamline and mainstream modern breeding approaches in RTB crops, aiming to increase genetic gains delivered on farmer’s fields.
● Achieve progress towards developing effective and efficient CGIAR-NARS breeding networks in RTB crops.
● Quickly transition from a “one crop” mindset towards a shared one, fostering and incentivizing cross-crop, cross-center and cross-national programs collaborations to better serve farmers.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: D-200352-SOILS4MED -Soil Health Monitoring and Information
Dscription: SOILS4MED -Soil Health Monitoring and Information Systems for Sustainable Soil Management in the Mediterranean Region
Project Summary: SOILS4MED engages a large platform of scientists, stakeholders and final users in the co-design of scientifically sound, sustainable, and policy-relevant integrated soil health monitoring systems harmonized across the Mediterranean region and will demonstrate the multiple societal benefits deriving from increased investment in soil data and information. This is implemented in line with the principles of the EU Mission A Soil Deal for Europe. The project will tackle the negative implications of the poor availability and use of Soil Data and Information (SDI) in decision processes. The project will further design sustainable and productive faming systems suited to soil conditions in the Mediterranean region which is facing increasing food security issues and declining small-farmer incomes.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: P-1743 SoLAR Phase 2
Dscription: Solar irrigation for Agricultural Resilience - Phase II
Project Summary: The program aims to strengthen the enabling environment and unlock investments for sustainable scaling of socially inclusive and climate-resilient solar agri-tech solutions in South Asia (India, Bangladesh) and East Africa (Ethiopia, Kenya). It will deliver across four main outcomes (OCs) that relate to Evidence-based policy design (OC1), Accelerating finance (OC2), Capacity Building (OC3) and Scaling Solar through Living Labs (OC4). Through this, the project will support improvement of energy and water security for vulnerable populations, help mitigate emissions from agriculture and increase adaptive capacity and resilience to climate change.
Project results:
The program outcomes will strengthen existing governance structures and systems and enable policies in the agriculture and energy sectors to ensure sustainable scaling and financing of solar energy for agriculture and food systems in South Asia and East Africa. The Project will deliver across four main outcomes in selected countries - South Asia (India, Bangladesh) and East Africa (Ethiopia, Kenya). The four outcomes together cover key elements needed to unpack barriers to scale solar energy for agriculture (SESA) - policy, finance, capacity and scaling (Figure 1). The resulting outputs and activities will work together synergistically in a logical and effective way, ensuring that the program goals and impact are achieved. Outcome 1 will develop decision-support tools and principles for effective scaling for policymakers, Outcome 2 will enhance these tools by introducing innovative financing options. However, without adequate capacity and buy-in from end-users, the challenges for scaling solar will persist - Outcomes 3 and 4 will leverage findings from Outcomes 1 and 2 to ensure that stakeholders are informed and equipped to utilize these innovations. Outcome 4 will focus on scaling and optimizing through existing platforms and living labs, such that sustainability of the innovations is well embedded beyond the timeframe of this program.

Tools, methodologies, and products from this phase will be co-designed and developed alongside partners who play leading roles in solar energy upscaling and policy development, and eventually will be embedded and hosted by stakeholders including government (e.g. Ministries such as Ministry of New and Renewable Energy of India, Ministry of Agriculture, Ethiopia) and implementing agencies (e.g. National Solar Help Desk of Bangladesh).

Name: W-D-0525- Subsidy Soil Fertility Programme - West Africa
Dscription: Subsidy Soil Fertility Programme - West Africa
Project Summary: The Soil Values Project will ensure that sustainable soil fertility management will be a mainstay of Sahelian and Guinea Savanna farming systems, improving the soil fertility and productive capacity of 2 million hectares of farmland in the Sahel and the resilience and well-being of 1.5 million small-scale food producers, particularly women and youth, in four implementation countries: Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, and Northern Nigeria.
Project results: The Soil Values project will ensure that sustainable soil fertility management will be a mainstay of Sahelian and Guinea Savanna farming systems, improving the soil fertility and productive capacity of 2 million hectares of farmland in the Sahel. It will build the resilience and well-being of 1.5 million small-scale food producers, particularly women and youth, in the four implementation countries, Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, and Northern Nigeria.

Name: C0172 Sustainable Intensification of Smallholder Farming Systems (SIFAZ) in Zambia
Dscription: Sustainable Intensification of Smallholder Farming Systems (SIFAZ) in Zambia
Project Summary: The Sustainable Intensification of Smallholder Farming Systems in Zambia (SIFAZ) project aims to enhance productivity and sustainability in Zambian smallholder farming. Implemented by FAO, CIMMYT, and the Ministry of Agriculture, SIFAZ focuses on applying research to promote sustainable and climate-smart practices in three agro-ecological zones. The project tests and scales up sustainable intensification practices, including mechanization, while fostering market linkages and supporting Zambia's Vision 2030 goals for a competitive, export-led agriculture sector. Funded by the EU from 2019 to 2026, CIMMYT leads adaptive research and field trials across southern, eastern, and northern Zambia.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: W-D-0467-EU - Balochistan WR Programme
Dscription: Technical Assistance for the Revival of the Balochistan Water Resources Programme
Project Summary: The Revival of Balochistan Water Resources Programme is a European Union-funded initiative of USD 39.9 million. It is designed to contribute to the transition of agriculture and livestock farming systems in Balochistan achieving lower water intensity and improving environmental and economic sustainability. The Programme is implemented through two distinct but complementary implementing contracts working closely together under a project steering committee. The technical assistance component in Balochistan will contribute to: a) strengthening the governance of water resources and rangelands; b) developing alternative agricultural and livestock farming systems based on low water agro-economy; and c) increasing the availability of qualified agro-technology cadres. The consortium is led by Landell Mills. IWMI’s USD 5 million component includes the implementation of 4 field research studies. IWMI’s Project Leader is Dr Mohsin Hafeez.
Project results:
This programme is designed to contribute to the transition of agriculture and livestock farming systems in Balochistan achieving lower water intensity and improving environmental and economic sustainability. The program is implemented through two distinct but complementary implementing contracts working closely together under a project steering committee.

The technical assistance component in Balochistan will contribute to; strengthening the governance of water resources and rangelands; developing alternative agricultural and livestock farming systems based on low water agro-economy; and increasing the availability of qualified agro-technology cadres.

Name: Climate-smart initiatives for climate change adaptation and sustainability in prioritized agricultural production systems in Colombia (CSICAP)
Dscription: The Climate-Smart Initiatives for Climate Change Adaptation and Sustainability in Prioritized Agricultural Production Systems in Colombia (CSICAP) project aims to safeguard livelihoods, boost productivity, and enhance profitability in nine of Colombia's key agri-food value chains, while increasing resilience to climate change impacts and reducing greenhouse gas emissions in the agriculture sector. With a USD $100 million budget, funded by the Green Climate Fund (GCF), Development Bank of Latin America (CAF), the government of Colombia, and partner contributions, CSICAP aims to benefit 200,000 people directly and nearly one million indirectly. The project will help reduce over 2 million tons of CO2 equivalent emissions through digital agroclimatic services, climate-resilient crops, water-efficient climate-smart practices, inclusive capacity-building, and innovative business models. It will be implemented in 22 out of 32 departments in Colombia.
Project Summary: The Climate-Smart Initiatives for Climate Change Adaptation and Sustainability in Prioritized Agricultural Production Systems in Colombia (CSICAP) project aims to safeguard livelihoods, boost productivity, and enhance profitability in nine of Colombia's key agri-food value chains, while increasing resilience to climate change impacts and reducing greenhouse gas emissions in the agriculture sector. With a USD $100 million budget, funded by the Green Climate Fund (GCF), Development Bank of Latin America (CAF), the government of Colombia, and partner contributions, CSICAP aims to benefit 200,000 people directly and nearly one million indirectly. The project will help reduce over 2 million tons of CO2 equivalent emissions through digital agroclimatic services, climate-resilient crops, water-efficient climate-smart practices, inclusive capacity-building, and innovative business models. It will be implemented in 22 out of 32 departments in Colombia.
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: D-200086-Ultra-Low Energy Drip Irrigation for MENA Countries
Dscription: Ultra-Low Energy Drip Irrigation for MENA Countries
Project Summary: Not available yet
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: T-PJ-003942-IITA Accelerated Innovation Delivery Initiative (AID-I) for Mozambique
Dscription: USAID/IITA - IITA ACCELERATED INNOVATION DELIVERY INITIATIVE (AID-I) FOR MOZAMBIQUE (RAFS)-PJ-3942...S.BOAHEN
Project Summary: This initiative seeks to boost food and nutrition security, incomes and resilience for smallholder farmers in Mozambique's Nampula, Zambézia and Niassa provinces. It aims to scale proven agricultural and nutritional innovations to tackle low productivity, extreme weather impacts and global supply chain disruptions. This program focuses on improving seed systems, enhancing village livestock productivity, integrating soil and crop management, increasing access to nutrient-dense foods and expanding market access and credit. Targeting 750,000 smallholder farmers, this initiative emphasizes gender equality, social inclusion and capacity development. By addressing these key areas, this project intends to increase productivity and profitability, ultimately improving the livelihoods of farmers and their communities.
Project results: Improved functionality of seed systems that cater to the needs of smallholder farmers, leading to increased productivity, enhanced market access, and greater availability of nutrient-dense foods.

Name: L-KOR025-The Ministry of Agriculture,Food and Rural Affairs
Dscription: The Ministry of Agriculture,Food and Rural Affairs of the Republic of Korea
Project Summary:
This project uses a One Health approach to tackle zoonotic diseases by strengthening capacities of national partners in surveillance, early detection and emergency response, disease prevention and control, and laboratory science.
This project uses One Health to ensure a multidisciplinary and multi-sectoral approach to addressing the threat of zoonotic diseases in Vietnam. Key activities include i) Conduct gap analysis of the current registration of transboundary animal and emerging zoonotic diseases (disease prioritization process using a One Health approach), ii) Establish One Health transboundary animal and emerging zoonotic diseases surveillance working groups and set up networks among them and with key surveillance partners, iii) Conduct a pilot study for a surveillance and monitoring system using ICT responding to transboundary animal and emerging zoonotic diseases with authorities and relevant stakeholders, iv) Deploy tools and methods to improve the surveillance of transboundary animal and emerging zoonotic diseases and v) Develop strategies to improve agricultural extension services and share equal benefits and rights for women through gender-focused interventions
We partner with Vietnam ministries of agriculture (MARD), of Health (MOH), university of public Health and NARS and provincial One Health partners such as animal and public health workers.
This project is mapped to One Health and data and digital areas of work.

Name: L-BMZ002-One Health Research, Education and Outreach Centre
Dscription: One Health Research, Education and Outreach Centre for Africa (OHRECA)
Project Summary: OHRECA supports applied research and capacity building to generate new knowledge, develop skills and identify incentives that would strengthen One Health policies and practices for the prioritized health challenges in Africa. Its activities are clustered into three work packages including generating actionable evidence on hazards, intervention implementation and assessments, and networks and partnerships. Capacity building is included as a cross-cutting activity. By 2025, the project will have (a) developed food safety benchmarking will be implemented by EAC across 7 countries in Africa, (b) updated National Action plans on AMR in Uganda, Kenya and Malawi, (c) strengthened One Health platforms strengthened in 8 OHRECA countries, and (d) designed One Health approaches for Key One Health challenges in Kenya, Uganda, Mali and Burkina Faso. The project is funded by BMZ, and its main partners include governments in the target countries, VSF Germany Malteser International and LUANAR.

Name: L-MML008-Fleming Fund Country Grant for Kenya phase 2
Dscription: Fleming Fund Country Grant for Kenya phase 2
Project Summary: By integrating climate science with practical solutions, the project seeks to build adaptive capacity in African agricultural systems. It emphasizes the co-creation of knowledge with local communities and stakeholders, ensuring that research outputs are contextually relevant and widely adopted. The initiative also aims to influence policy and investment decisions, creating an enabling environment for the widespread implementation of climate-resilient practices across the continent.

Name: Enhancing Crop Productivity and Climate Resilience for Food and Nutrition Security in Mali (FNS-Mali)
Dscription: Enhancing Crop Productivity and Climate Resilience for Food and Nutrition Security in Mali
Project Summary: The project aims to demonstrate that circular food systems can decouple growth in social and economic benefits from consumption of natural resources (particularly water and land) and production of greenhouse gas emissions in agriculture, reducing urbanization by creating local opportunities for the women and youth. We will test how smallholder irrigation schemes might become the engine of local resilience and economic development by being integrated with livestock, and dryland agricultural production to support strategic and inclusive development of small, value-adding business to form climate smart circular food systems in East and Southern Africa.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: D-200371-Development and Scaling of Introduced Plant Species
Dscription: Development and Scaling of Introduced Plant Species for Food and Feed Crops with a Functional Seed Delivery System in Abu Dhabi
Project Summary:
The main objective of this project is to increase the availability and to ensure the sustainability of high value feed and food crop production with drought, heat, and salinity tolerant species for different agroecological zones in Abu Dhabi. 
The studies are to be carried through participatory adaptive research in a systemic mode of operation covering the different aspects of soil, water, climate, plant, seed, livestock and socio-economic factors. The studies aim to investigate in conventional and alternative fodder and food crop productivity under the prevalent desert farming ecosystems of Abu Dhabi, covering the basic crop management practices under the prevalent biotic and abiotic constraints of water, soil fertility, pests and socio-economic conditions.
By the end of the project, the project research team will release a report consisting of a list of adapted germplasms to the Abu Dhabi environmental conditions with the associated agriculture inputs, agronomic package, the appropriate post-harvest operations, biomass and seed yields, feed quality attributes, socioeconomic benefits and value chain analysis.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: BIUSA (BEF)-Screening, developing, and deploying anti-methanogenic feedstock into livestock systems in the Global South
Dscription:
Description and project summary both filled. The "Screening, Developing, and Deploying Anti-Methanogenic Feedstock into Livestock Systems in the Global South" project aims to reduce methane (CH4) emissions from livestock, the single largest emitter in the agricultural sector, by leveraging the potential of novel compounds in CGIAR’s global collections of forages. This initiative involves screening, developing, and deploying anti-methanogenic feedstock into livestock systems in the Global South to achieve a significant reduction in greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, equivalent to taking 7 million internal combustion engine cars off the road each year. Scope and Activities:
1. Discovery (WP1):
o Screen CGIAR gene bank collections to identify accessions with high levels of anti-methanogenic compounds (AMCs).
o Conduct metabolomics analyses to identify known and novel AMCs.
o Validate the anti-methanogenic activity of selected accessions through in vivo and in vitro assays.
2. Development (WP2):
o Promote the use of AMC-rich forage legumes directly in grazing systems.
o Enhance AMC levels in widely used forage-grass cultivars through conventional breeding and gene-editing techniques.
3. Deployment (WP3):
o Establish public-private partnerships for large-scale multi-environment feeding/grazing trials.
o Develop Monitoring, Reporting, and Verification (MRV) systems for AMC-rich forages.
o Facilitate the adoption of AMC-rich forages in target regions.
Project Summary: The "Screening, Developing, and Deploying Anti-Methanogenic Feedstock into Livestock Systems in the Global South" project targets a significant reduction in methane emissions from livestock. By leveraging CGIAR’s global collections of forages, the project will screen, develop, and deploy anti-methanogenic feedstock, aiming to mitigate greenhouse gas emissions on a large scale. The project involves three main phases: discovery of AMC-rich forages, development of these forages through breeding and gene-editing, and deployment in livestock systems through public-private partnerships. Major outcomes include reduced methane emissions, improved livestock productivity, and enhanced sustainability of agricultural practices in the Global South
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: F-PP-2022-1020-Climate-Resilient Aquaculture Systems for Afr (AG10618)
Dscription: Climate-Resilient Aquaculture Systems for Africa (CASA)
Project Summary: WorldFish and partners are Implementing the “Climate-Resilient Aquaculture Systems for Africa - CASA” in Kenya and Nigeria. CASA delivers solutions that simultaneously increase productivity and profitability, climate adaptation and environmental sustainability, food and nutrition security, and the expansion of employment and income opportunities within the aquaculture systems. CASA has four Interlinked Intervention Areas (IAs). Under IA1, CASA will deliver income and productivity gains for most of the farmers, with an estimated 1x - 2x increase in productivity for the average farmer per cycle, and the best-performing farmers obtaining 3x increase in productivity per cycle. Under IA2, CASA aims to reach 1,000 fish processors with improved, climate-smart fish processing technologies and practices. Under IA3, CASA will support the development and piloting of financial products and services which target the aquaculture value chains. Under IA4, CASA will support the strengthening of the policy environment and the capacity development of policymakers.

Name: L-BMG026-CGIAR Livestock Partnership
Dscription: CGIAR Livestock Partnership
Project Summary:
This project aims to establish new products and technologies for tropical livestock; enhance resource allocation and policies at national and state levels; and strengthen global policy, planning, accountability, and resources for livestock sector development. Key components include:

Global Livestock Advocacy for Development: Addresses the negative perception of livestock, low sector attention, and lack of advocacy.
African Dairy Genetic Gains: Main tool for dairy performance recording, integrating genomic selection and AI delivery.
Transforming Animal-Health Solutions and Services for LMICs: Unites ILRI, ClinGlobal, and GALVmed to create a one-stop shop for R&D, product development, and registration.
Policy Options for Livestock: Provides technical guidance for developing Livestock Master Plans and improving ILRI’s technical capabilities.
Gender: Consolidates data on gender issues in livestock programming and prioritizes actionable investments.

Name: L-UOE012-Centre for Tropical Livestock Genetics and Health -
Dscription: Centre for Tropical Livestock Genetics and Health - Phase 2 (FCDO-DF)
Project Summary:
The Centre for Tropical Livestock Genetics and Health (CTLGH) is a collaborative initiative between the International Livestock Research Institute (ILRI) and the University of Edinburgh. It focuses on improving the productivity and resilience of livestock in tropical environments through advanced genetics and genomics. The project aims to develop livestock breeds that are better adapted to tropical climates, more resistant to diseases, and more productive, thereby supporting the livelihoods of small-scale farmers and pastoralists in the Global South.

CTLGH works on identifying genetic traits that enhance livestock performance and resilience, leveraging cutting-edge technologies such as gene editing and genomic selection. The project also emphasizes capacity building and knowledge transfer, working closely with local and international partners to ensure the practical application of research findings. By integrating science with development goals, CTLGH seeks to enhance food security, economic stability, and environmental sustainability in tropical regions.

Name: L-EUR021-Capacitating One Health in Eastern and Southern Afr
Dscription: Capacitating One Health in Eastern and Southern Africa (COHESA)
Project Summary:
Operating in 12 countries across Eastern and Southern Africa (ESA), COHESA is establishing society-wide OH capacity to rapidly address complex threats that impact the health of humans, animals and environments.
Objectives-Activities:
• Knowledge Sharing: Share knowledge of OH initiatives, research and policies – Achieved through assessments of OH landscape in 12 countries, and a virtual One Health Observatory.
• Governance: Enhanced collaboration between government entities and stakeholders – Achieved by advancing inter-departmental government OH platforms, and strategies.
• Education: Educational and research institutes equipped to train the next generation workforce in tackling OH issues – Achieved by expanding professional, university and school level OH education.
• Delivery: Increased capacity to deliver OH solutions – Achieved by tackling specific OH challenges through model approaches, informed by action-research.
