Name: P-1547-MAP0-Fortalecimiento de la conservación y uso sosteni
Dscription: Fortalecimiento de la conservación y uso sostenible de variedades locales de raíces y tubérculos andinos libres de enfermedades en la Zona de Agrobiodiversidad Andenes de Cuyocuyo - Puno
Project Summary: Under the Strategic Alliance Agreement between the National Institute of Agrarian Innovation (INIA) and the International Potato Center (CIP), the project aims at  repatriating to peasant communities cultivars of native potatoes from the CIP germplasm bank. It also aims at strengthening the management and the morphological characterization of native potatoes and other Andean Roots and Tubers (RTAs) by farmers. Overall objective is to contribute to improved information on crops diversity.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: T-PJ-004023-VACS-Breeding: TARO
Dscription: PJ-004023: VACS-Breeding: TARO
Project Summary: This project aims to enhance agrifood systems by developing and delivering productive, climate-resilient and nutrient-dense taro varieties in Nigeria, Ghana, Ethiopia and Mozambique. The primary objectives are to boost productivity, improve nutritional content and commercialize climate-resilient taro varieties for smallholder farmers. Key outputs of this project include defined taro market segments, established breeding networks, detailed germplasm reports and robust breeding systems. Additionally, this project will produce a significant quantity of seeds, train numerous individuals and strengthen market linkages among taro farmers. Activities in this project include conducting workshops, identifying market segments, developing product profiles and characterizing taro germplasm.

Name: T-PJ-004017-VACS-Breeding: Bambara
Dscription: PJ-004017: VACS-Breeding: Bambara
Project Summary: This project aims to enhance the development and dissemination of Bambara groundnut varieties through a gender-responsive approach that prioritizes nutrition traits. By establishing the PAN African Bambara Breeding Network (PABBN), the initiative will facilitate collaboration and knowledge sharing across regions. Germplasm development will be guided by target product profiles, ensuring that the resulting varieties meet the specific needs of different communities, thereby improving food security and resilience across Africa.
Project results: Establish Bambara breeding networks, defined market segments, target product profiles, set up breeding systems (BMS) and characterized germplasm. To also deliver MET, on-farm and DUS reports, produce seeds, train personnel, submit progress reports, analyze sales data, strengthen market linkages and release high-yielding, resilient and nutrient-dense Bambara varieties for commercialization.

Name: T-PJ-003575-Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptat
Dscription: Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptation in Cassava from CGIAR gene banks

Name: V0181-Programa de Agricultura Regenerativa
Dscription: Programa de Agricultura Regenerativa
Project Summary: The Regeneration and Conservation of High-Altitude Maize Accessions in Mexico project is a collaborative initiative between CIMMYT and ICAMEX, aimed at strengthening genetic conservation efforts and enhancing maize biodiversity in the Valles Altos region of the State of Mexico. This initiative focuses on regenerating, characterizing, and incorporating maize accessions into the ICAMEX Germplasm Bank, ensuring the long-term preservation and use of native maize varieties. Key activities include seed regeneration in experimental fields, technical training for ICAMEX personnel, infrastructure improvement for seed conservation, and the development of standardized operating procedures for germplasm management. The project integrates scientific research, capacity-building, and public-sector collaboration to establish sustainable genetic conservation frameworks. By promoting biodiversity conservation and improving seed availability, this initiative supports food security, climate resilience, and the sustainable development of Mexico’s agricultural sector.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: R0127-GCDT-LTG Wheat
Dscription: GCDT-LTG Wheat
Project Summary: The Long-Term Funding for Wheat Germplasm Conservation project is a partnership between CIMMYT and the Global Crop Diversity Trust (GCDT) to ensure the sustainable conservation and availability of wheat genetic resources. This initiative provides long-term financial support for CIMMYT’s ex situ wheat germplasm collection, aligning with the International Treaty on Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture. The project focuses on secure storage, management, and distribution of wheat genetic materials, supporting global food security and climate adaptation efforts. Key activities include safety duplication, germplasm characterization, digital documentation, and strengthening links between genebanks and users. By maintaining high standards for genetic conservation, the initiative ensures that farmers, breeders, and researchers worldwide have access to diverse wheat genetic resources to develop improved, resilient crop varieties. This collaboration contributes to sustainable agriculture, biodiversity preservation, and the resilience of global food systems.
Center: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center / Centro Internacional de Mejoramiento de Maíz y Trigo

Name: R-A-2024-3-2024-2028LPA
Project Summary: This project supports the International Rice Genebank in the conservation, characterization and utilization of plant genetic resources (rice).

Name: T-PJ-003576-Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptat
Dscription: Mining useful alleles for climate change adaptation in cowpea from CGIAR gene banks
Project results: Identify, validate, and deploy climate-adaptive genomic variants in elite germplasm using Environmental Genome-Wide Association Studies (EnvGWAS) to develop climate-resilient maize, sorghum, rice, cowpea, and cassava with enriched Quantitative Trait Locus (QTL) profiles, validated markers, and optimized breeding methodologies, benefiting farmers, especially women.

Name: 1571-FAO0 - Youth, Citizen Science and E-commerce: scaling integrated conservation solutions and farmers’ rights by connecting key diversity hotspots: Bolivia, Chile, and Peru
Dscription: Youth, Citizen Science and E-commerce: scaling integrated conservation solutions and farmers’ rights by connecting key diversity hotspots: Bolivia, Chile, and Peru
Project Summary: The main objective of this project is to scale integrated conservation solutions and farmers’ rights by connecting key diversity hotspots in Bolivia, Chile, and Peru. The aim is to generate intergenerational dialogue towards the effective conservation of genetic resources (potato, maize and quinoa), to document and monitor diversity, and to provide different types of incentives to custodian farmers.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: Conservation of ICRISAT genetic resources for food and nutrition security in the semi-arid tropics
Dscription: To support a number of priority Article 15 PGRFA collections at ICRISAT
Project Summary: The main aim of the project is safeguarding the ex-situ collection of PGRFA, held by ICRISAT. This funding enables ICRISAT genebank to meet the performance targets of essential genebank operations and tasks that the Crop Trust has established for the long-term conservation germplasm resources of dryland millets and legumes. Key output includes germplasm regeneration, multiplication, characterization, monitoring viability, health testing, and capacity building, towardings achiving performance targets.
Center: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics

Name: D-100735-Barley quality traits
Dscription: Revealing the Diversity of Barley Quality Traits through Synergies between On-farm Practices and Technological Innovations
Project Summary: The project employs innovative, smart, and user-friendly digital tools that will link novel generated information on barley quality traits with already documented genotypic data. It focuses its activities on three countries with a long tradition of barley crop production: Ethiopia, Morocco, and Tunisia. Its ultimate purpose is to explore the barley quality traits of different landraces grown in these countries under on farm management systems and, to complement this information with nutritional quality and genomic data from the ICARDA ex-situ barley collection and to certify selected landraces from these countries for elite quality genotypic profile that suits to specific use. This will improve the barley production in the targeted 3 countries and enhance the local value chains of the adapted varieties.
Center: International Center for Agricultural Research in the Dry Areas

Name: 1572-MIPO  Impulsando la Agroindustria Rural y el Uso de la Diversidad del Yacón conservada en Pataz y CIP, a través de la Bioeconomía y los Alimentos Funcionales
Dscription: Promoting Rural Agribusiness and the use of Yacon diversity conserved in Pataz and CIP, through the Bioeconomy and Functional Foods
Project Summary: To improve the economic income, health and quality of life of people linked to the yacon value chain in Peru, with greater emphasis on the district of Pataz (La Libertad department) and its area of influence.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: T-PJ-003670- Long-term funding of Ex Situ collections of germplasm held by IITA
Dscription: Long-term funding of Ex Situ collections of germplasm held by IITA
Project Summary: The Long-term Partnership Agreement (LPA) application is for the Genetic Resources Centre (GRC) seedbank at IITA, focused on conserving and distributing a diverse collection of cowpea, soybean, maize and underutilized legumes. Objectives include maintaining high performance in seed conservation and distribution, with recent improvements in PDCI and safety duplication. Key outputs include the completion of a new building floor, expanded seed processing areas and enhanced staff training. The GRC has fostered collaborations with national genebanks in sub-Saharan Africa and advanced its greening efforts with solar panels. Funding support from OneCG and BMGF for climate-smart traits complements the LPA.
Project results: This partnership will enhance capacity of IITA’s genebanks to preserve and manage a wider variety of crops, directly contributing to global food security and agricultural resilience.

Name: L-LTG001-Long term funding of ex situ collections of germpl
Dscription: Long term funding of ex situ collections of germplasm

Name: R-A-2023-9-Genebank-AI
Dscription: Genebank-AI
Project Summary: A combination of AI and high-throughput phenotyping methodologies will be applied to characterize the IRG collection for tolerance to flooding, drought and salinity. Seeds of varieties will be systematically bulked to generate one composite sample. The bulked samples will be evaluated in high-throughput screening facilities. By employing AI, the identity of the original variety will be established from the seeds of surviving plants. The results will be confirmed in subsequent seasons. An AI tool that identifies ~60,000 IRG samples is available; this will be scaled to identify ~132,000 samples.

Name: GCDT-Long-term conservation and sustainable use of plant genetic resources
Dscription:
Description and project summary both filled. The Long-term Partnership Agreement (LPA) between the Global Crop Diversity Trust (Crop Trust) and the Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical (CIAT) aims to ensure the secure conservation and availability of Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture (PGRFA). This agreement focuses on supporting the long-term maintenance of PGRFA collections, promoting sustainable agriculture and global food security. The partnership is established within the framework of the International Treaty on Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture and the Global Plan of Action for the Conservation and Sustainable Utilization of Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture. Scope and Activities:
1. Conservation and Maintenance:
o Sustain genebank operations to support the funded collections.
o Maintain performance targets including safety duplication, availability, and documentation of PGRFA.
2. Capacity Building and Collaboration:
o Provide capacity-building support to national agricultural research systems (NARS) partners.
o Collaborate with international institutions to strengthen the global system of ex situ PGRFA conservation.
3. Financial and Technical Support:
o Crop Trust provides Essential Operations Funds based on the agreed Business Plan.
o CIAT provides Matching Funds as described in the Business Plan.
4. Monitoring and Evaluation:
o Regularly review and update the Business Plan.
o Submit annual budgets, workplans, technical, and financial reports through an online reporting tool.
Project Summary: The Long-term Partnership Agreement (LPA) between the Global Crop Diversity Trust (Crop Trust) and the Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical (CIAT) focuses on the secure conservation and sustainable utilization of Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture (PGRFA). This project aims to maintain and enhance genebank operations, ensure the availability and safety of PGRFA, and build capacity within national agricultural research systems. By supporting the long-term maintenance of PGRFA collections, the project promotes sustainable agriculture and global food security. Major outcomes include improved conservation practices, strengthened international collaboration, and enhanced capacity for PGRFA management.
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: BIUSA (BEF)-Screening, developing, and deploying anti-methanogenic feedstock into livestock systems in the Global South
Dscription:
Description and project summary both filled. The "Screening, Developing, and Deploying Anti-Methanogenic Feedstock into Livestock Systems in the Global South" project aims to reduce methane (CH4) emissions from livestock, the single largest emitter in the agricultural sector, by leveraging the potential of novel compounds in CGIAR’s global collections of forages. This initiative involves screening, developing, and deploying anti-methanogenic feedstock into livestock systems in the Global South to achieve a significant reduction in greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, equivalent to taking 7 million internal combustion engine cars off the road each year. Scope and Activities:
1. Discovery (WP1):
o Screen CGIAR gene bank collections to identify accessions with high levels of anti-methanogenic compounds (AMCs).
o Conduct metabolomics analyses to identify known and novel AMCs.
o Validate the anti-methanogenic activity of selected accessions through in vivo and in vitro assays.
2. Development (WP2):
o Promote the use of AMC-rich forage legumes directly in grazing systems.
o Enhance AMC levels in widely used forage-grass cultivars through conventional breeding and gene-editing techniques.
3. Deployment (WP3):
o Establish public-private partnerships for large-scale multi-environment feeding/grazing trials.
o Develop Monitoring, Reporting, and Verification (MRV) systems for AMC-rich forages.
o Facilitate the adoption of AMC-rich forages in target regions.
Project Summary: The "Screening, Developing, and Deploying Anti-Methanogenic Feedstock into Livestock Systems in the Global South" project targets a significant reduction in methane emissions from livestock. By leveraging CGIAR’s global collections of forages, the project will screen, develop, and deploy anti-methanogenic feedstock, aiming to mitigate greenhouse gas emissions on a large scale. The project involves three main phases: discovery of AMC-rich forages, development of these forages through breeding and gene-editing, and deployment in livestock systems through public-private partnerships. Major outcomes include reduced methane emissions, improved livestock productivity, and enhanced sustainability of agricultural practices in the Global South
Center: Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT - Regional Hub (International Center for Tropical Agriculture / Centro Internacional de Agricultura Tropical)

Name: P-1530-GIZ0-Construction and implementation of a new Cryoban
Dscription: Construction and implementation of a new Cryobank - establishing the Center of Excellence in Crop Cryopreservation for Latin America
Project Summary: The conservation of valuable Plant Genetic Resources (PGR) is directly related to the achievement of the UN’s Strategic Development Goals (SGDs). SDG 2 (End Hunger), target 2.5, focuses on the maintenance of genetic diversity of cultivated plants and their wild relatives, including through soundly managed and diversified seed and plant banks at the national, regional, and international level. Potato is the third global crop in terms of consumption (Devaux et al., 2020), whereas sweetpotato is quickly becoming a main global crop because of its continuous growth, particularly in Sub Saharan Africa and Asia in recent years. Both crops are clonally propagated and due to that, they are mainly conserved in vitro.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa

Name: P-1560-DGUL-Andean Crop Diversity for Climate Change
Dscription: Andean Crop Diversity for Climate Change
Project Summary: This project aims to strengthen the resilience of Andean agriculture by leveraging traditional potato and mashua varieties and gene bank resources. Through an integrated and interdisciplinary approach, climate adaptation will be promoted, and food security and incomes improved. Applied methods will involve participatory evaluations, nutritional analyses, market appraisals, genotyping, and restoration of lost biodiversity. Transformational change will be achieved through the integration of research, knowledge sharing, innovations, and enhanced market access. We will employ a comprehensive communication strategy to share key findings and foster policy engagement. The project will create lasting benefits by endorsing diversity conservation, catalysing transformational change, and applying advanced agricultural technologies.
Center: International Potato Center / Centro Internacional de la Papa
